BI - RBD SFE Metric List.xlsx.heading: Columns: Index, Metric, Definition
BI - RBD SFE Metric List.xlsx.text: Columns: Index, Metric, Definition: Index: 1 | Metric: Account Calls & Frequency (Target vs non target) | Definition: Calls - Total product calls delivered to target/non target accounts in the quarter Frequency - Average number of product calls per HCA to target/non target accounts in the quarter. Index: 2 | Metric: Account reach (Target vs non target) | Definition: % Accounts reached out for product promotion out of the brand target list in the quarter (at any detail position) Index: 3 | Metric: HCP calls & Frequency (Target vs non target) | Definition: Calls - Total product calls to target/non target HCPs in the quarter Frequency - Average number of product calls to reached target/non target HCPs in the quarter. Index: 4 | Metric: HCP reach (Target vs non target) | Definition: % HCPs reached out for product promotion out of the brand target list in the quarter (at any detail position) Index: 5 | Metric: # Speaker Events | Definition: # Speaker program events conducted for the product in the quarter (Status – Closed, Completed) Index: 6 | Metric: Branded vs Non branded promotional events | Definition: # of branded vs non branded promotional event conducted in the quarter. Index: 7 | Metric: PEP vs. P2P promotional events | Definition: # of Patient Education and Peer to Peer programs for the product conducted in the quarter. Index: 8 | Metric: # Displays & Exhibit Events | Definition: # D&E events for the product conducted in the quarter. Index: 9 | Metric: Proportion of Effort | Definition: % of total calls made to a specific tier or target segment compared to total calls.

BI - RBD SFE Metric List.xlsx.heading: Columns: Index, Metric, Definition
BI - RBD SFE Metric List.xlsx.text: Columns: Index, Metric, Definition: Index: 9 | Metric: Proportion of Effort | Definition: % of total calls made to a specific tier or target segment compared to total calls. Index: 10 | Metric: Rep triggered email | Definition: Delivered RTE - Total Emails Triggered by rep Opened RTE - Delivered Emails that were opened Avg Open Item - Average number of times and RTE was opened.

dcv_metric_definition.xlsx.section_title: DCV QS Metric Def
dcv_metric_definition.xlsx.text: Sheet: DCV QS Metric Def Rows With Content: 60 Data Rows: 59 Worksheet Max Row: 64 Worksheet Max Column: 7 Max Populated Column Index: 7.

dcv_metric_definition.xlsx.section_title: Columns: Metric Definition
dcv_metric_definition.xlsx.heading: Columns: Metric Definition
dcv_metric_definition.xlsx.text: Columns: Metric Definition: Metric Definition: Category | C: KPIs | D: Business Definition | E: Granularity | F: Time period | G: Calculation. Metric Definition: Sales Performance (Xponent) | C: TRx Volume | D: Total number of prescriptions written by a physician | E: geography,product, segment, specialty, week | F: R4/R13/QTD/Current week | G: Sum(TRx) C: TRx Attainment | D: Total Prescriptions written as compared to the goal | E: geography,product,week | F: QTD | G: Sum(TRX)/TRx Goal. C: Share Deviation | D: Difference between Total prescriptions written for a product/total prescriptions written for all the products in the market and the share goal | E: geography,product,week | F: QTD | G: Market Share Actual-Share Goal. C: TRx Goal | D: Weekly gated IC volume goal - Received from IC team | E: geography,product, segment, specialty,Week | F: QTD | G: For Toujeo : Weekly gated goals obtained from IC. C: Remaining Goals | D: Difference between Total number of prescriptions written by a physician and Weekly gated IC volume goal | E: geography,product | F: QTD | G: Sum(TRX)-TRx Goal. C: TRx share Goal | D: IC share goal defined at a product level - Received from IC team | E: Geography, product,week | F: QTD | G: Max(Goal_mkt_shr)

dcv_metric_definition.xlsx.section_title: Columns: Metric Definition
dcv_metric_definition.xlsx.heading: Columns: Metric Definition
dcv_metric_definition.xlsx.text: Columns: Metric Definition: C: Trx Share | D: Ratio of Total prescriptions written for a product to total prescriptions written for all the products in the market | E: geography,product, segment, specialty,Payor,Formulary Access | F: R4/R13/QTD | G: TRxMarketShare=Sum(TRx)Selected Product/Sum(TRx)All Products. C: TRx Market Volume | D: Total number of prescriptions written by a physician for all the products with in a market. | E: geography, segment, Specialty | F: R4/R13 | G: sum(TRx) C: TRx Market Contribution | D: E.g.: Contribution by Payor : Ratio of Total prescriptions for one Payor to total prescriptions for all the payors. | E: Payor,Channel,formulary Access,Coverage,Prescription | F: R4/R13 | G: TRxMarketContribution=Sum(TRx)For one payor/Sum(TRx)All Payorss. C: TRx Volume growth | D: Total number of prescriptions written by a physician in current cycle compared against previous cycle/Total number of prescriptions written by a physician in previous cycle | E: geography, product, segment, specialty | F: R4/R13 | G: (sum(Trx)[current]-sum(Trx)[Previous])/sum(TRx)[Previous] C: Share change | D: Total prescriptions written for a product/total prescriptions written for all the products in the market compared between current and previous cycle | E: Geography, segment, specialty, product,Tier | F: R4/R13 | G: Current share-previous share.

dcv_metric_definition.xlsx.section_title: Columns: Metric Definition
dcv_metric_definition.xlsx.heading: Columns: Metric Definition
dcv_metric_definition.xlsx.text: Columns: Metric Definition: C: % Access of Praluent in advantage and parity | D: Percentage of total no. of prescription written for the payors having coverage Parity and exclusive with respect to the total no. of prescriptions | E: Payor | F: R4/R13 | G: Sum(TRx)Coverage=Parity or Exclusive/Sum(TRx) C: Total Writers | D: Total number of physician who have a TRx in the selected time period | E: Geography,Segment, Speciality,Week | F: QTD,Current Week | G: Count(HCP)TRx<>0. C: New Writers | D: For Praluent, any physician who has not written in Q1'17 and only started writing in Q2'17 is a new writer. Prescription history of 2016 is irrelevant. For Soliqua, any physician who has written for the first time since launch(6th Jan, 2017) is a new writer. | E: Geography,Segment, Speciality,Week | F: QTD,Current Week | G: Count(HCP)TRx<>0 since Q217. C: Repeat Writer | D: Any physician who has written in current week and also wrote in one or more of the last 4 weeks is a repeat writer | E: Geography,Segment, Speciality,Week | F: QTD,Current Week | G: Count(HCP)TRx<>0 for last 4 weeks. C: Inactive Writer | D: Any physician who has not written a script for the last 8 weeks is an inactive writer | E: Geography,Segment, Speciality,Week | F: QTD,Current Week | G: Count(HCP)TRx=0 for last 8 weeks.

dcv_metric_definition.xlsx.section_title: Columns: Metric Definition
dcv_metric_definition.xlsx.heading: Columns: Metric Definition
dcv_metric_definition.xlsx.text: Columns: Metric Definition: C: Dabbler Writer | D: Any physician who has written in the current week as well as one other week but it was longer than 4 weeks ago is a dabbler | E: Geography,Segment, Speciality,Week | F: QTD,Current Week | G: Count(HCP)TRx<>0 for weeks longer than 4. C: Trx/Writers | D: Ratio of Total number of prescriptions written by a physician to Total number of physician who have a TRx in the selected time period | E: Geography | F: QTD,Current Week | G: Sum(TRx)/Count(HCP)TRx<>0. C: Target Penetration | D: Percentage of praluent targets who have written praluent prescription | E: Geography,Writer Category | F: QTD. C: Current Enrollment Volume | D: Number of patients enrolled for Praluent treatment | E: Geography, Product | F: R4/R13. Metric Definition: Flex and Speaker Programs | C: Flex Events | D: No of flex events conducted by product | E: Geography, product | F: QTD | G: Count(Distinct Events) C: Flex Goals | D: Flex Event Goals defined at a product level | E: Geography, product | F: QTD | G: Sum(Flex_Goals) C: Speaker Programs | D: # of speaker programs conducted at nation level by product | E: Geography, segment, specialty, product | F: QTD | G: Count(Speaker Programs)

dcv_metric_definition.xlsx.section_title: Columns: Metric Definition
dcv_metric_definition.xlsx.heading: Columns: Metric Definition
dcv_metric_definition.xlsx.text: Columns: Metric Definition: C: Reach (Flex Events) | D: % targets with flex events in the call plan time period | E: Geography, segment, specialty, product | F: QTD | G: Targets with Flex events/Total Targets *100. C: Reach (Speaker Programs) | D: % targets with speaker programs in the call plan time period | E: Geography, segment, specialty, product | F: QTD | G: Targets with Speaker programs/Total Targets *100. C: Speaker Attainment | D: Ratio Speaker programs conducted to Goal | E: Geography,product | F: QTD | G: count(events)/goal. C: Flex Attainment | D: Ratio Flex conducted to Goal | E: Geography, product | F: QTD | G: count(events)/goal. Metric Definition: Execution and Samples | C: Calls | D: Sum total of Calls done to any Physicians for any product | E: Geography,product | F: QTD | G: Count(Distinct Calls) where pde is not null. C: Planned Calls | D: Total no. of calls to be made as per the call plan | E: Geography,product | F: QTD | G: Count(Distinct Calls) where pde is not null. C: Pharmacy Calls | D: Total no. of calls to be made to pharmacy targets for any product | E: Geography,product | F: QTD | G: Count(Distinct Calls to pharmacy) where pde is not null. C: Samples | D: Total Number of Samples delivered | E: Geography,product | F: QTD | G: Sum(Samples Delivered)

dcv_metric_definition.xlsx.section_title: Columns: Metric Definition
dcv_metric_definition.xlsx.heading: Columns: Metric Definition
dcv_metric_definition.xlsx.text: Columns: Metric Definition: C: Samples | D: Total Number of Samples delivered | E: Geography,product | F: QTD | G: Sum(Samples Delivered) C: Planned Samples | D: Total number of samples planned to be delivered | E: Geography,product | F: QTD | G: Sum(Planned Samples) C: Samples to Target | D: Total Number of Samples delivered to target customers | E: Geography,product | F: QTD. C: Call Reach/Target Reach | D: It is the % of targets who were called out of the entire call plan/target list | E: Geography, product, Segment, Specialty | F: QTD | G: Targets Called/Total Targets *100. C: Sample Reach | D: It is the % of targets to whom samples were dropped out of the entire sample plan/target list | E: Geography, product, Segment, Specialty | F: QTD | G: Targets with Samples/Total Targets *100. C: Call Plan Adherence (CPA) | D: It is the sum of (minimum of either the calls delivered or the planned(apportioned) calls for each physician) in the selected geography/ the planned(apportioned) calls. Example in next sheet | E: Geography, product, Segment, Specialty,Tier | F: QTD | G: min(planned pde,actual pde)/planned pde. C: CPA Goal | D: CPA Goal adjusted with the vacancy rate of selected geography. The Goal is floored at 0% | E: Geography | F: QTD | G: x=85% - Vacancy rate of selected geography If x<0, then 0. Else x.

dcv_metric_definition.xlsx.section_title: Columns: Metric Definition
dcv_metric_definition.xlsx.heading: Columns: Metric Definition
dcv_metric_definition.xlsx.text: Columns: Metric Definition: C: Sample Plan Adherence (SPA) | D: It is the sum of (minimum of either the samples delivered or the planned(apportioned) samples for each physician) in the selected geography/ the planned(apportioned) samples. Example in next sheet | E: Geography, product, Segment, Specialty | F: QTD | G: min(planned samples,actual samples)/planned samples. C: Sample attainment | D: Samples dropped to targets/planned samples to targets | E: Geography, product, Segment, Specialty | F: QTD | G: sum(samples) to targets/sum(planned samples) C: Sample Plan Utilization | D: Total Number of Samples delivered/Total number of samples planned to be delivered | E: Geography,product | F: QTD | G: Sum(Samples Delivered)/Sum(Planned Samples) C: P2 Completion Rate | D: total of Calls done to any Physicians for any product detailed at secondary position/Sum total of Calls done to any Physicians for any product | E: Geography,product | F: QTD | G: secondarycalls/actualcalls. C: Vacancy Rate | D: No of days territory was vacant/finance days | E: Geography | F: QTD | G: sum(vacancy)/finance days. C: Calls per day | D: It is calculated as total calls divided by total no. of working days in all the territories | E: Geography, product, Segment, Specialty | F: QTD | G: sum(calls)/sum(working days)

dcv_metric_definition.xlsx.section_title: Columns: Metric Definition
dcv_metric_definition.xlsx.heading: Columns: Metric Definition
dcv_metric_definition.xlsx.text: Columns: Metric Definition: C: Calls per day to target | D: It is calculated as total no. of target calls divided by total no. of working days in all the territories. | E: Geography, product, Segment, Specialty | F: QTD | G: sum(calls to call plan targets)/sum(working days) C: Working Days | D: The number of days the sales rep has recorded 1 or more calls | E: Geography | F: QTD. C: No of targets | D: Its number of target customers of the selected geography with non zero call frequency present in call plan/target list | E: Geography, product, Segment, Specialty | F: QTD | G: Count(Distinct call plan targets) for selected geography and product. C: No of targets Called | D: Number of Call Plan customers who were called | E: Geography, product, Segment, Specialty | F: QTD. C: Calls to target | D: It is the no. of calls made to physicians by selected team who are present in the call plan/target list | E: Geography, product, Segment, Specialty | F: QTD | G: Sum (calls) to call plan targets for selected geography and product. C: Planned PDE | D: It is the total no. of apportioned PDEs that were planned for the physician. This metric will be 0 when call plan is not there | E: Geography, product, Segment, Specialty | F: QTD | G: Sum(PDE)Planned Flag=1.

dcv_metric_definition.xlsx.section_title: Columns: Metric Definition
dcv_metric_definition.xlsx.heading: Columns: Metric Definition
dcv_metric_definition.xlsx.text: Columns: Metric Definition: C: Actual PDE | D: Total PDEs delievered by the Rep. For primary, secondary and tertiary detail position, PDE is 1, 0.5 and 0.1 respectively. PDE is 0 for all other detail positions. | E: Geography, product, Segment, Specialty | F: QTD | G: Sum(PDE) C: PDE Gap | D: It is the difference between Total PDE delivered by the Rep and apportioned planned PDE | E: Geography, product, Segment, Specialty | F: QTD | G: Sum(PDE)-Sum(PDE)Planned Flag=1. C: PDE to target | D: It is the no. of PDEs delivered to the physicians who are a part of the call plan/target list | E: Geography, product, Segment, Specialty | F: QTD | G: Sum(PDE)Targets. C: PDE to non Target | D: It is the no. of PDEs that were delivered to physicians who were not a part of the call plan/target list for selected geography | E: Geography, product, Segment, Specialty | F: QTD | G: Sum(PDE)Non Targets. C: Meeting/Training Days | D: Number of days the sales rep has taken a time off in the category (The number of hours is converted into days) + Number of days the sales rep has taken a time off in the category (The number of hours is converted into days) | E: Geography | F: QTD. C: Vacation/Personal days | D: Number of days the sales rep has taken a time off in the category (The number of hours is converted into days) | E: Geography | F: QTD.

dcv_metric_definition.xlsx.section_title: Columns: Metric Definition
dcv_metric_definition.xlsx.heading: Columns: Metric Definition
dcv_metric_definition.xlsx.text: Columns: Metric Definition: C: Vacancy | D: Number of days the territory was vacant | E: Geography | F: QTD. C: % of Available Days Worked | D: Number of days the sales rep has recorded 1 or more calls as a ratio of QTD call plan days. This has vacancy rate factored for calculation | E: Geography | F: QTD | G: Sum(Working Days)/Call Plan Days - (Vacancy Days*Call Plan Days/Finance Days) C: Adherence | D: 1) Depending on how many times a target was called when compared to planned PDE, adherence is decided 2) If a target is called 80-120% of the planned PDE, it is optimally called. Above it is over called and below it is under called 3) If the total no. of PDE is less than 10, there are seperate product specific rules | E: Geography, product, Segment, Specialty | F: QTD | G: x=Sum of PDE to targets/Planned PDE x>=0.8 or x<=1.2 => Optimally Called x<0.8 => Under Called x>1.2 => Over Called.

Dupixent Field Dashboard_HelpBox_v6.0.pp.text: [image] Primary KPIs will reflect your respective territory’s Performance Metrics. Logged-in user’s territory code and name will be displayed here. Navigate to the Sales Data Inquiry Form. **Note: Filters and data on the view will be subset based on your assigned Sales team. Metric Definitions.

QTD Volume | Units sold in Quarter Till Date time period at Territory Level
Nat. QTD Vol. | Units sold in Quarter Till Date time period at Nation Level
LTD Volume | Units sold in Launch Till Date time period at Territory Level
Nat. LTD Vol. | Units sold in Launch Till Date time period at Nation Level
QTD Sales Attainment | Ratio of Units sold in QTD time period to its respective QTD goal at Territory Level
Nat. Sales Attn. | Ratio of Units sold in QTD time period to its respective QTD goal at Nation Level
Growth (13) | % Growth calculated at rolling 13 weeks time period
Previous QTR Volume | Units sold in Previous Quarter at Territory Level
Growth (vs Prev QTR) | % Growth calculated at QTD Time Period
Vol Change (vs Prev QTR) | Volume Change calculated at QTD Time Period
[image] Click Here for more filters. Navigate to the Sales Data Inquiry Form. **Note: Filters and data on the view will be subset based on your assigned Sales team. Metric Definitions.

P13W % Patients PSP Util. | % of In Network Rx’d patients with PSP enrollment in previous 13 weeks

QTD <Segment> Freq. | Ratio of Total number of interactions to the total number of target customers for the specific <Segment>

Date, Prepared By, Reviewed By, Approved By, Affected Section & Summary of Change Row 1: 1, 03/09, Vikram Gupta, Manikandan Jayaraman, Manikandan Jayaraman, Requirement specification of Medical Reporting Dashboard Row 2: 2, 04/22/19, Rishabh Wadhwa, , , Requirement specification of Medical Reporting Dashboard(HCA + HCP) Table of Contents. 1. Scope 4. 2. Business Rules 4. 3. KPIs 5. 4. Qlik Sense views 8. 5. Appendix 16.

Medical Report_Requirement Specification.section_key: Scope
Medical Report_Requirement Specification.section_title: Scope
Medical Report_Requirement Specification.heading: Scope
Medical Report_Requirement Specification.text: Scope: The Scope of this document included the Phase-I Qlik Sense implementation of the Medical report. The dashboard shows the lifecycle of an interaction. How the medical service liaison reaches out to a HCP/HCA, how the information is shared in terms of an Interaction or a Presentation, the details being discussed, the mindset of the HCP/HCAs and getting the information and tracking the journey of the various inquiries (MIRs) sent by the HCP/HCAs for a grievance. Data Sources: Veeva, CIM, Field Deployment.

section_path: Data Sources: Veeva, CIM, Field Deployment
Medical Report_Requirement Specification.section_key: Data Sources: Veeva, CIM, Field Deployment
Medical Report_Requirement Specification.section_title: Data Sources: Veeva, CIM, Field Deployment
Medical Report_Requirement Specification.heading: Data Sources: Veeva, CIM, Field Deployment
Medical Report_Requirement Specification.text: Columns: Data Source, Information Captured Row 1: Veeva, Calls – Call Details- Call Discussion- Product- User- Product Metrics- Medical Events- Medical Inquiry- Account-Medical Quarter Row 2: CIM, Customer Profiles, Source-Territory, Product Row 3: Field Deployment, Sales Team to Business Unit mapping. Business Rules.

Medical Report_Requirement Specification.section_key: Business Rules
Medical Report_Requirement Specification.section_title: Business Rules
Medical Report_Requirement Specification.heading: Business Rules
Medical Report_Requirement Specification.text: Columns: Rule, Rule/Description Row 1: Time, Starting from July 2017 to the latest week’s data Row 2: Time, Both Medical HCA and HCP reports are refreshed on a weekly basis Row 3: User Security, Section Access based on geographical assignment of medical team Row 4: Sales Teams - HCP, Row 5: Sales Teams - HCA, Row 6: Business Units, Diabetes, CV, Biosurgery, Oncology, Endo, MS, Rare Disease, Genetic Disease etc. Row 7: Customer Universe, All Affiliated Physicians and Accounts – Current Profiles Row 8: Calls, No Call Only/Sample Only calls Call Status should be in Submitted Only Row 9: KOL/NKOL, Engage the product metrics with customer profile table to drive these values, if a customer has a flag 1, then it would be Key opinion leader, otherwise not. KPIs.

Medical Report_Requirement Specification.section_key: KPIs
Medical Report_Requirement Specification.section_title: KPIs
Medical Report_Requirement Specification.heading: KPIs
Medical Report_Requirement Specification.text:
Columns: KPIs, Business Definition, Calculation
Row 1: # Interactions (2-Way), # of Unique HCP Interactions = #Interactions KOL + #Interactions Non-KOL
# of Unique HCA Interactions = #Interactions Non-KOL
All Activity corresponding to following Record Type Names:
Event_vod, Interactions, Medical Non-Verified
MSL Meeting Brief_vod, Count(Distinct Call ID) where Record Type Name in ("Event_vod",
"Interactions","Medical Non Verified",
"MSL Meeting Brief_vod")
Row 2: # Interactions KOL, No of Interactions to KOL Accounts, Count(Distinct Call ID) where KOL Flag=1 and Record Type Name in ("Event_vod",
"Interactions", "Medical Non Verified",
"MSL Meeting Brief_vod")
Row 3: # Interactions Non-KOL, No of Interactions to Non-KOL Accounts, Count(Distinct Call ID) where KOL Flag=0 and Record Type Name in ("Event_vod",
"Interactions",
"Medical Non Verified",
"MSL Meeting Brief_vod")
Row 4: # Interaction prod Touchpoints, Total number of people the MSL had a 2-way exchange with X the total products/disease states discussed, (#Interaction Prod Touchpoints KOL + #Interaction Prod Touchpoints Non-KOL) x products/disease states discussed
Row 5: # Interaction Touchpoints KOL, All unique Interaction engagements corresponding to KOL accounts and products, # Interactions with KOL x # Prod Discussed
Row 6: # Interaction Touchpoints Non-KOL, All unique Interaction engagements corresponding to Non-KOL accounts and products, # Interactions with Non-KOL x # Prod Discussed

Medical Report_Requirement Specification.section_key: KPIs
Medical Report_Requirement Specification.section_title: KPIs
Medical Report_Requirement Specification.heading: KPIs
Medical Report_Requirement Specification.text:
Columns: KPIs, Business Definition, Calculation
Row 7: # Presentation (1-Way), Unique Presentation Meetings (i.e. Only parent meeting)
All Activity corresponding to:
Record Type Name: "Presentation", Count(Distinct Parent Call ID) where KOL Flag=1 and Record Type Name="Presentation"
Row 8: # Presentation Attendees, Number of attendees entered in Veeva Medical (number value), Sum(# Presentation Attendees)
Row 9: # Presentation Attendees KOL, #Presentation Attendee as KOL, Count(Distinct Call ID) where Record Type Name="Presentation" and KOL Flag=1
Row 10: # Presentation Attendees Non-KOL, #Presentation Attendee as Non-KOL, Count(Distinct Call ID) where Record Type Name="Presentation" and KOL Flag=0
Row 11: # Presentation Attendees Others, # of Unique Presentation Instances corresponding to Non-Profiled attendees, (# Presentation Attendees) - (# Presentation Attendees KOL + # Presentation Attendees Non-KOL)
#Presentation Attendee (Non-Profiled)
Row 12: # Presentation Prod Touchpoints, Total number of people the MSL had a 1-way exchange with X the total products/disease states discussed, Total Number of Presentation Attendees entered in Veeva Medical (number value) x # Prod Discussed
Row 13: # Presentation Touchpoints KOL, All unique Presentation engagements corresponding to KOL accounts and products, # Presentation with KOL x # Prod Discussed
Row 14: # Presentation Touchpoints Non-KOL, All unique Presentation engagements corresponding to Non-KOL accounts and products, # Presentation with Non-KOL x # Prod Discussed

Medical Report_Requirement Specification.section_key: KPIs
Medical Report_Requirement Specification.section_title: KPIs
Medical Report_Requirement Specification.heading: KPIs
Medical Report_Requirement Specification.text:
Columns: KPIs, Business Definition, Calculation
Row 15: # Presentation Touchpoints Others, All unique Presentation engagements corresponding to Non-profiled accounts and products, Total # of Presentations Other (2d above) x # Prod Discussed
Row 16: # Total Interact/Present Attendees, Total number of people with whom the MSL interacted, (# Interactions) + (# Presentation Attendees)
Row 17: # Total Interact/Present Attendees KOL, (Total Number of Presentation Attendees entered in Veeva Medical (number value)), #Interactions Attendees + #Presentation Attendees
Row 18: # Total Interact/Present Attendees Non-KOL, # of Unique Interaction or Presentation Instances corresponding to Non-KOL accounts, (# Interactions Non-KOL) + (# Presentation Attendees Non-KOL)
Row 19: # Total Interact/Present Attendees Others, Calculates only presentation attendees as all interactions are against profiled attendees, #Presentation Attendee as Others (Non Profiled)
Row 20: # Product Touchpoints, Total number of people the MSL interacted with x the total products/disease states discussed, #Prod Touchpoints KOL + #Prod Touchpoints Non-KOL + #Prod Touchpoints Others
Row 21: # Product Touchpoints KOL, All unique engagements corresponding to KOL accounts and products, Total Interact/Present KOL (#Interaction Prod Touchpoints KOL + #Presentation Prod Touchpoints KOL) x # Prod Discussed
Row 22: # Product Touchpoints Non-KOL, All unique engagements corresponding to Non-KOL accounts and products, Total Interact/Present Non- KOL (#Interaction Prod Touchpoints Non-KOL + #Presentation Prod Touchpoints Non- KOL) x # Prod Discussed

Medical Report_Requirement Specification.section_key: KPIs
Medical Report_Requirement Specification.section_title: KPIs
Medical Report_Requirement Specification.heading: KPIs
Medical Report_Requirement Specification.text:
Columns: KPIs, Business Definition, Calculation
Row 23: # Product Touchpoints Others, All unique engagements corresponding to Non-profiled accounts and products, Calculates only presentation attendees as all interactions are against profiled attendees. Total # of Presentations Other (2d above) x # Prod Discussed
Row 24: Unique KOL Reached, No of KOL physicians who have been called, Count(Distinct CCT ID) where (KOL Flag=1 and Call ID<>NULL)
Row 25: Unique N-KOL Reached, No of N-KOL physicians who have been called, Count(Distinct CCT ID) where (KOL Flag=0 and Call ID<>NULL)
Row 26: # MIR Requests, No of medical Inquiries, Count(Distinct MIR ID) where status in Closed MSL, Closed MIS, Saved and Open
Row 27: HCP Interactions by Month, # Calls to physicians by Month, Count(Distinct Call ID) per Quarter
Row 28: % Interactions Aligned to Strategy, % Calls by Strategic Imperative, Count (Distinct Call ID) per Strategy / Total Count(Distinct Call ID)
Row 29: # Closed MIR Requests, No of medical Inquiries that are closed/resolved, Count(Distinct MIR ID) where status in Closed MSL, Closed MIS
Row 30: # Saved MIR Requests, No of medical Inquiries that are saved, Count(Distinct MIR ID) where status in Saved
Row 31: # Open MIR Requests, No of medical Inquiries that are open and are yet to be resolved, Count(Distinct MIR ID) where status in Open
Row 32: Interactions by Product, # Calls by Product, Count (Distinct Call ID) per Product / Total Count(Distinct Call ID)

Medical Report_Requirement Specification.heading: KPIs
Medical Report_Requirement Specification.text: Columns: KPIs, Business Definition, Calculation Row 33: Interactions by Activity Type, # Calls by Activity type, Count (Distinct Call ID) per Activity Type/ Total Count(Distinct Call ID) Row 34: Interactions by Channel, # Calls by Channels, Count (Distinct Call ID) per Channel/ Total Count(Distinct Call ID) Row 35: Medical Requests by Channel, # MIRs by Channel, Count(Distinct MIR ID) per Channel Row 36: Medical Requests by Product, # MIRs by Product, Count(Distinct MIR ID) per Product Row 37: MIR Trend by Quarter, # MIRs by Quarters, by Status, Count(Distinct MIR ID) per Quarter Row 38: # Responses, Number of responses to a Survey Question, Count({<[Statement Value Description]-={''}>}[CCT ID]) Row 39: % Response, % of a Responses for each survey question, Count({<[Statement Value Description]-={''}>}[CCT ID])/count({<[Statement Value Description]-={''}>}total <[Survey Name],Question>[Statement Value Description]) Qlik Sense views.

Medical Report_Requirement Specification.section_title: HCP Application
Medical Report_Requirement Specification.heading: HCP Application
Medical Report_Requirement Specification.text: HCP Application: Home Screen – Holistic information about the interactions, presentations, KOLs, Non-KOLs and MIRs. The first level in the tree map is the sales team, followed by 4 levels of geography hierarchy in addition, filters have been provided on the filter pane for various metrics like Time-period, product, HCA/HCP flag, customer names, employee names etc.

Medical Report_Requirement Specification.text: Purpose of the Document: This document captures detailed user requirements to build a tool to enhance Business Analytics capabilities of Sanofi team to analyze the Medical Reporting Ecosystem, by leveraging the centrally developed and supported CIM system and building an Ad-hoc analytics layer on top of it. This new Ad-hoc Analytics layer would be based on Qlik Sense platform and would be primarily developed for Sanofi team to carry out different analysis including field leadership, Regional Medical Liaisons, HQ staff and senior executives. Meeting with Jacob - Diamond Journey and.heading: Diamond evolved through deliberate trade-offs made to meet urgent business priorities while progressively building toward enterprise-ready foundation.
Meeting with Jacob - Diamond Journey and.text:
Diamond evolved through deliberate trade-offs made to meet urgent business priorities while progressively building toward enterprise-ready foundation.

[image] From rapid launch enablement to commercial data foundation. Rapid platform enablement to support critical brand launch when legacy systems could not scale. Diamond expanded into a multi-domain integration layer supporting additional therapeutic areas. Platform transitioned from single-brand solution to enterprise onboarding engine. Diamond becomes the commercial data foundation across brands and products. 2022–23. 2023–24. 2024–25. 2025–26. Legacy CIM/SGDW unable to process new brand data volumes. Growing downstream dependencies required operational stability. Multi-brand onboarding and launch readiness became primary priority. Enterprise integration mandate across GenMeds, IC, and FD. [image] [image] [image] [image] Immediate commercial timelines required delivery within ~18 months. [image] [image] Business adoption increased faster than original scope. Standard onboarding pathways introduced to accelerate scale. Strategic decision to sunset legacy CIM processing. [image] [image] Priority placed on launch readiness over enterprise redesign. Organizational and leadership transitions reduced appetite for large re-architecture. Incremental evolution favored over disruptive redesign to protect operations. [image] Platform maturity enables enterprise standardization phase. [image] [image] [image]

Outcome Delivery Took Priority Over Structural Optimization. Change Management Cost Influenced Technical Decisions. Limited Central Enterprise Data Architecture Function.

Meeting with Jacob - Diamond Journey and.text: Diamond evolved through deliberate trade-offs made to meet urgent business priorities: 2022–23: Urgent Build. 2023–24: Stabilize & Expand. 2024–25: Scale & Enable. 2026: Modernized Enterprise. Business Trigger Legacy system (CIM/SGDW) unable to process Dupixent data (constant issues and failures) Requirement for diamond platform to onboard Dupixent processes and share process outbounds to CIM/SGDW in legacy format No formal discovery phase to identify cross brand requirements. Business Context Integration of different data domains like sales, patient, CRM etc. for 3 more TAs - RBD, Oncology, Kevzara Increasing downstream dependencies led to lower appetite for change management and overall Retrofitment Leadership changes (e.g., departure of Susan) altered decision authority. Business Context Multi-brand onboarding: Dupixent, Rare Disease, Tzield, MS Tolebrutinib on the existing platform Launch readiness requirements. Business Context Integration of GenMeds, IC, FD Mandate to sunset CIM/SGDW. Architectural Trade-Off Diamond positioned as landing & transformation layer for all the onboarding brands Maintained downstream data formats to align with data management requirements Data contracts not enforced enterprise-wide Key Inflection Operational continuity prioritized over data rationalization Decommissioning of legacy system post go live of each brand. Strategic Outcome Diamond becomes commercial data warehouse for all Brands/Products Complete retirement of Legacy CIM Processing (Q3’26) Architectural Decision Designed for rapid onboarding to cater Dupixent specific business needs Narrow scope aligned to a single brand requirement not for entire Commercial portfolio Key Inflection Architecture shaped for a specific product, not enterprise modeling (brand agnostic)

Meeting with Jacob - Diamond Journey and.text:
Diamond evolved through deliberate trade-offs made to meet urgent business priorities while progressively building toward enterprise-ready foundation.

Guiding Principles – Deliver Launch Readiness First, Business Continuity, Scale Progressively. Rapid platform enablement to support critical brand launch when legacy systems could not scale. Diamond expanded into a multi-domain integration layer supporting additional therapeutic areas. Platform transitioned from single-brand solution to enterprise onboarding engine. Diamond becomes the commercial data foundation across brands and products. 2022–23. 2025–26. 2023–24. 2024–25. Business Imperatives. Legacy CIM/SGDW unable to process new brand data volumes Immediate commercial timelines required delivery within ~18 months Priority placed on launch readiness over enterprise redesign. Multi-brand onboarding and launch readiness became primary priority Standard onboarding pathways introduced to accelerate scale Incremental evolution favored over disruptive redesign to protect operations. Enterprise integration mandate across GenMeds, IC, and FD Strategic decision to sunset legacy CIM processing Platform maturity enables enterprise standardization phase. Growing downstream dependencies required operational stability Business adoption increased faster than original scope Organizational and leadership transitions reduced appetite for large re-architecture.

Meeting with Jacob - Diamond Journey and.heading: Issues and Reasons
Meeting with Jacob - Diamond Journey and.text:
Theme | Description | Impact
Speed vs. Sustainability | The platform was developed under tight timelines (≤ 1.5 years) with no formal discovery phase.
The urgent onboarding of Dupixent took priority over enterprise re-architecture.
Design decisions were optimized for rapid deployment, not long-term scalability or standardization. | The system met immediate business needs but accumulated architectural constraints that limit flexibility and modernization.
Brand Urgency vs. Enterprise Standardization | The architecture was shaped primarily by the needs of Dupixent, rather than a holistic enterprise model.
Dupixent could not be processed in the legacy system, requiring a fast-track solution.
Coding and logic were often tailored by Therapeutic Areas (TAs)
Enterprise-wide data modeling (sum of all needs approach) was not completed. | The platform became brand-driven rather than standardized, creating fragmentation across Therapeutic Areas (TAs).
Operational Continuity vs. Data Rationalization | Maintaining business continuity outweighed structural data cleanup.
Downstream systems required data in the same format.
Full data rationalization would have broken existing integrations.
Data contracts were not enforced across the board.
The sunsetting of CIM/SGDW/DSS accelerated migration without redesign time. | Diamond operated primarily as a landing and transformation layer, rather than a fully rationalized enterprise warehouse.
Directive Decisions vs. Architectural Vision | Governance dynamics influenced architectural outcomes.
Some decisions were directive rather than architecturally driven.
Proposed improvements were highlighted but not fully executed.
Leadership changes (e.g., departure of Susan) altered decision authority.
High change management overhead discouraged re-architecture. | Strategic architectural evolution slowed, reinforcing tactical delivery choices.

Meeting with Jacob - Diamond Journey and.heading: Issues and Reasons
Meeting with Jacob - Diamond Journey and.text:
S. No. | Issue | Description
1 | Source Data Rationalization | TA wise Roadmap to enable diamond, leading to lack of clarity of all expected future data sources
Priority to enable Dupixent Foundation in diamond, focusing on Dupixent design rather than designing the Commercial Warehouse
Requirements to use same source data as legacy reducing the opportunity to rationalize feeds from different products
Diamond was a base system and source for multiple parallel systems like Turing, Sapphire. Thus, diamond layouts were in sync with the requirements for parallel systems as well
2 | Downstream Specific redundant datasets | Due to TA-by-TA Integration, Any changes to existing production deliverables led to additional change management overhead
Timeline Crunch, due to pre aligned legacy decommissioning timelines focus was on timely delivery instead of robust and scalable design
3 | Data Architecture | Data Layers initial aligned with the Sanofi DE Team, was reviewed and aligned but due to committed timelines the design was never looked back or retrofitted to improve

PSS A&R Data Products Proposal v1.pptx.heading: Approach: Detailed activities and ownership
PSS A&R Data Products Proposal v1.pptx.text: Approach: Detailed activities and ownership: Requirements Gathering & Onboarding. Requirements Mapping. Design and Data Modelling. Implementation and Testing. Validation. Hypercare and Downstream Retrofitment. Map gathered Dupixent business requirements to their data sources and data grain. Oversight support of 6 weeks for providing guidance on dashboard retrofitment Provide retrofitment SOPs and Accelerators. Create a Business Requirements Document & Update Dupixent requirements in the BRD. ZS. Based on the mapped requirements, identify the archetypes of Consumer Aligned Datasets (CADs) and Reporting Ready datasets (RRDs) needed and their data granularity. Develop high-level architecture, Logical and Physical data model, and ER diagram of the CADs and RRDs to support the requirements and build phase. Validation of CAD/RRD schemas with pre-defined PAD data model Development, Testing and Deployment of CADs & RRDs Update the documents based on final deliverables Designing the Playbook for DevOps Governance and Operating Model. Produce sample outputs for validation Conduct UAT with respective stakeholders address feedback and obtain sign-off from all downstream consumers. 3-week Hypercare: Post-go-live operations support, defect fixing, and issue triage. Co-Owned (ZS+ICI) Fill Business Requirement Document template for ICI Downstream use cases PSS A&R Onboarding to existing ICI Data Model. Map gathered downstream business requirements to their data sources and data grain. ICI. Participate in UAT and provide feedback and sign-off. Retrofitment of PSS dashboards (8-9 weeks) with ZS oversight (for 6 weeks)

PSS A&R Data Products Proposal v1.pptx.heading: Project Assumptions (1/2)
PSS A&R Data Products Proposal v1.pptx.text: Project Assumptions (1/2): Requirements & Inputs Assumptions PSS A&R and ICI team to provide complete, accurate, and timely business requirements for non-Dupixent TAs and downstream consumers using the ZS Business and Data requirements template ZS will receive all necessary contextual information on use cases, KBQs, KPIs, and downstream reporting needs Any changes or additions to requirements may impact timelines and effort post the requirements phase closure Requirements from PSS A&R and ICI will be signed off before build begins Dependencies on upstream systems (e.g., source data owners) will be managed by Sanofi teams. Data Availability & Quality Assumptions Required source data for finalized CADs and RRDs will be available and accessible to Design and Development team Data issues (missing fields, unexpected values, quality problems) will be resolved by Digital/Source System owners All the relevant infrastructure, systems/database accesses, schema-level accesses will be provided by Sanofi Digital to support the project Data Validations will depend on representative sample outputs being provided by Digital.

PSS A&R Data Products Proposal v1.pptx.heading: Project Assumptions (1/2)
PSS A&R Data Products Proposal v1.pptx.text: Project Assumptions (1/2): Design & Build Assumptions ZS Team will be onboarded on to the existing ICI Data Model and PADs and design of up to 2 CADs and 10 RRDs are assumed as part of scope ZS and ICI to ensure that the proposed Data Products will be scalable across TAs and markets Design inputs (BRD, KPI mapping, metadata, etc.) will be treated as source of truth during implementation Any architectural constraints or sign-offs (OneMesh, iCare Insights ecosystem) will be clarified with Digital team Platform (iCare Insights, Snowflake, PBI ecosystem, etc.) is stable, accessible, and managed by Digital including DevOps The metadata creation for the CADs and RRDs will include – table and attribute names and detailed descriptions, along with ontologies/tags in English for 2 CADs and 10 RRDs.

PSS A&R Data Products Proposal v1.pptx.heading: Project Assumptions (2/2)
PSS A&R Data Products Proposal v1.pptx.text: Project Assumptions (2/2): Downstream Retrofitment Assumptions PBI dashboard retrofitment to be performed by Sanofi Hub team, timeline assumed is 8-9 weeks for the same ZS provides guidance, SOPs, and oversight and other guidance on hands-on retrofitment for initial 6 weeks Validation & Testing Assumptions The estimates are based on the assumption that ZS and ICI will be co-responsible for designing the CADs, STTMs, development of code, unit testing, and end to end testing of scoped CADs/RRDs All defects must be logged, triaged and resolved jointly UAT coordination, environment readiness, and user access are owned by Sanofi Digital UAT sign-off timelines depend on stakeholder availability from PSS A&R Governance & Collaboration Assumptions Cross-team governance meetings will be scheduled consistently (weekly/bi-weekly sessions + milestone reviews). Decision logs and risk logs maintained by ZS will be accepted as formal artifacts. Timeline & Estimation Assumptions Major requirement changes, new KPIs, new data sources, or new TAs may require timeline and cost adjustments. Parallel analytics workstreams will continue independently without impacting this program. Thankyou.

PSS A&R PSS Enhancements 2026 Proposal v.heading: Scope of Engagement
PSS A&R PSS Enhancements 2026 Proposal v.text: Scope of Engagement: On-therapy MPR, PDC & Inactive Rate pending build, SIT, UAT & launch along with 2 weeks of hyper care Update Executive summary to include different timeframe (CM vs. PM, C3M vs. P3M etc.) Separate out Persistency tab as standalone dashboard and include MPR tab in On Therapy Dashboard Add Avg Fill / Avg Length metric Visual & BR Enhancements Persistency – Drop-off Reason: This is a new enhancement. Onboarding dashboard Update executive summary tab to include different timeframe cuts (CM vs. PM, C3M vs. P3M, CW vs. PW, etc.) 2-3 ad-hoc enhancements every month Add Payer Name View Add SP Name View Visual Funnel to show patient volume change from initiation to conversion Incorporate SP Conversion Rate Incorporate Operational Conversion Rate Patient ID export per group Add Active Copay filter Add eRX views/ filters (such as fax vs. eRX enrollment channel, etc.) Add Age View – Nice to Have Add Patients N-size bar graph for Time to Fill with lookback – Nice to Have US State Heatmap – Nice to Have Reorganize the color threshold – Nice to Have.

Customer: Title of Project: Digital Partnered PSS A&R KPI Enablement through ICI Snowflake. Customer Project Contact: Primary: Aude Dassier, Head of Digital Insights & Analytics. Business Partner: Sandhya Sayani, Sr. Director. Vendor Project Contact: Business Partner: Prasoon Kumar, Principal Digital Partner: Gagan Bhatara, Principal The following terms and conditions apply solely to work performed under this IPA and do not apply to any other IPA. The IPA is governed by the terms of the Master Service Agreement dated February 22, 2021, as amended between Sanofi US Services Inc. and ZS Associates, Inc. (“MSA”). To the extent there is any conflict between the terms and conditions of this IPA and the MSA, the MSA controls. All capitalized terms in this IPA will have the same defined meanings as set forth in the MSA. This IPA shall be effective upon acceptance of the purchase order.

PSS AR KPI Enablement through ICI Snowfl.heading: Project Summary:
PSS AR KPI Enablement through ICI Snowfl.text: Project Summary: Sanofi’s PSS A&R team is looking to evolve its analytics ecosystem to align with Digital’s OneMesh / Data Mesh principles, enabling scalable, reusable, and AI-ready data assets. The objective of this engagement is to design and develop Consumption-Aligned Data Assets (CADs) and Reporting Ready Datasets (RRDs). These data assets will be aligned with Digital’s OneMesh data principles and are AI-ready for future “Talk to Data” capabilities to enable OnePSS dashboards with built-in KPIs, metadata, and configurable business rules. We will be leveraging existing Process-Aligned Data Assets (PADs) owned and managed by Sanofi ICI team. ZS will partner with Sanofi’s Digital ICI and PSS A&R teams to collaboratively design, implement, validate, and transition CADs/RRDs supporting current and future analytics use cases across therapy areas. The project will commence on Feb 9th, 2026 and will be completed on June 26th, 2026.

PSS AR KPI Enablement through ICI Snowfl.section_key: Project Scope: > The following activities are in scope for this engagement > Requirements Gathering and Onboarding
PSS AR KPI Enablement through ICI Snowfl.heading: Project Scope
PSS AR KPI Enablement through ICI Snowfl.text: Project Scope: The following activities are in scope for this engagement. Requirements Gathering and Onboarding: Structured onboarding to the Sanofi analytics ecosystem, including existing Sanofi ICI-owned Process-Aligned Data Assets (PADs), underlying data models, architecture, data quality framework and documented design rationale. Documentation of PSS A&R Dupixent data requirements (use cases, key business questions (KBQs), KPIs, data grain) in a standardized BRD template. Other Therapeutic Areas and ICI downstream requirements to be filled in by Sanofi PSS A&R and ICI team respectively.

PSS AR KPI Enablement through ICI Snowfl.text: Archetyping & Design (Co-led; Design owned by ICI): Identification and finalization of the CADs and RRD archetypes based on approved requirements and finalized requirements-to-data mappings (Total 12 Data Assets: Assuming 6 Data Assets each by ZS Sanofi ICI team. Assuming 1CAD + 5RRDs each by ZS and Sanofi ICI team respectively) Co-design the target data architecture and develop high-level, logical, and physical data models for CADs and RRDs, aligned with the architecture and design principles of the existing data ecosystem and is scalable and robust. Define data granularity, entity relationships, KPI calculation logic, and reusable business rules across therapy areas.

PSS AR KPI Enablement through ICI Snowfl.text: Out of scope: Any new data ingestion, data transformations or data assets (CADs/RRDs) outside the scope of PSS A&R application requirements are not in scope. Data assets virtualization or semantic layer design, development is outside the scope of this program. Design and development of PADs/process aligned data assets is not in scope of this program. Development of any new PowerBI dashboards.

PSS AR KPI Enablement through ICI Snowfl.text: Redesign of existing PowerBI dashboards: Advanced analytics enablement, such as ML model pipelines, AI/GenAI use cases, or predictive simulation layers, will not be part of this program. Data operations or BAU support is not considered to be part of scope this would include activities such as incident monitoring, ongoing SLA tracking, ticket resolution or enhancements/DataOps support after transition closure.

PSS AR KPI Enablement through ICI Snowfl.heading: Assumptions
PSS AR KPI Enablement through ICI Snowfl.text: Assumptions: 10-12 Data Assets have been assumed as the scope of the project. All underlying activities in the phases - Design, development, testing, UAT and hypercare to be done in collaboration with Sanofi ICI and Sanofi PSS A&R teams (6 Data Assets (CAD & RRD) by ZS and 6 Data Assets by Sanofi ICI) PSS A&R Hub and Sanofi ICI team provide complete, accurate, and timely business requirements for Other TAs and ICI downstream consumers respectively using the ZS Business and Data requirements template. All requirements to be signed off before archetyping and finalization of CADs. Any changes or additions to requirements may impact timelines and effort post the requirements phase closure. Any significant delays in timelines due to dependencies would impact on the effort and would be discussed with the team. All relevant data sources are already ingested, reconciled, quality checked and available for design and development. Sanofi ICI team will onboard ZS team on the existing PADs and ICI DQ framework. All the existing PADs are available, reconciled, production grade and stable for being leveraged for CAD development. Sanofi ICI team will support identification and walkthrough of relevant PADs to enable requirements mapping. Any data quality issues in the PADs will be resolved by Sanofi ICI team. All the CADs will be designed, developed & tested in partnership with Sanofi ICI. Design inputs (BRD, KPI mapping, metadata, etc.) will be treated as source of truth during implementation. Any architectural constraints or signoffs (OneMesh, iCare Insights ecosystem) will be clarified with Sanofi ICI team.

PSS AR KPI Enablement through ICI Snowfl.chunk_id: PSS AR KPI Enablement through ICI Snowflake.docx::chunk_0019
PSS AR KPI Enablement through ICI Snowfl.file_name: PSS AR KPI Enablement through ICI Snowflake.docx
PSS AR KPI Enablement through ICI Snowfl.source_file: PSS AR KPI Enablement through ICI Snowflake.docx

PSS AR KPI Enablement through ICI Snowfl.heading: Assumptions
PSS AR KPI Enablement through ICI Snowfl.text: Assumptions: Any architectural constraints or signoffs (OneMesh, iCare Insights ecosystem) will be clarified with Sanofi ICI team. The metadata creation for the CADs will include table and attribute names and detailed descriptions, along with ontologies/tags in English for the finalized CADs. PBI dashboard retrofitment to be performed by Sanofi PSS Hub team, timeline assumed is 8-9 weeks for the same. ZS will provide oversight support, on hands-on retrofitment by Sanofi PSS HUB for initial 6 weeks. Decision logs and risk logs maintained by ZS will be accepted as formal artifacts. Parallel PSS A&R analytics workstreams will continue independently without impacting this program. Sanofi would be expected to provide a sign-off on the design and other technical documents in a fixed duration of 3 business days of ZS providing the final version of the documents​. All the relevant infrastructure, systems/database accesses, schema-level access will be provided by Sanofi ICI to support the project. The indicative tech stack assumed for this implementation is IICS, Snowflake, Control-M, DBT, Github. Final alignment on the tech stack will happen during initial 2-3 weeks of engagement​. 3 weeks of extended hyper care support is assumed post go-live which includes transition support to Sanofi ICI. Role of Scrum Master will be provided by the Sanofi ICI/Digital team. Sanofi stakeholders will provide an ongoing sign off for each project phase (Requirements, Design, Build, SIT, UAT, Go-Live) based on the respective deliverables for each phase.

PSS AR KPI Enablement through ICI Snowfl.text: Assumptions: Procurement of licenses (Snowflake, Power BI, IICS, DBT, Airflow, etc.) is excluded. ZS assumes these licenses are provisioned and managed by Sanofi prior to building. Any additional enhancement support to be scoped separately. Integration of data assets and metadata within CDGC data catalog will be handled by Sanofi internal teams.

PSS AR KPI Enablement through ICI Snowfl.text: Expectations from ICI Team in terms of team members: Requirements Gathering & Mapping: 1 FTE Business Analyst. PSS AR KPI Enablement through ICI Snowfl.heading: Key activities and deliverables
PSS AR KPI Enablement through ICI Snowfl.text: Key activities and deliverables: Columns: Key Activities, Details, Expected Outcomes/Deliverables Row 1: Onboarding and Requirements Gathering, Conduct formal kickoff sessions with Sanofi stakeholders to align objectives, scope, governance, and ways of working Establish communication plan, governance cadence (steering, working sessions), and initial project plan Provision access for the project team to required systems and tools (e.g., Snowflake, Informatica, Confluence, SharePoint) Finalize team structure, RACI, and onboarding plan for all stakeholders Structured onboarding to Sanofi ecosystem and review of existing ICI PAD data models, architecture, DQ framework and design rationale Gather requirements into a business requirement document template and validate business requirements from PSS A&R stakeholders, Onboarding: domain access, infrastructure and other tools and tech accesses set up for each team member. Established program governance cadence and operating model Duly filled and approved business requirements document for PSS A&R use cases (Dupixent) JIRA user stories Row 2: Requirements Mapping, Map approved PSS A&R business requirements to available source systems and existing ICI-owned PADs, including identification of data grain and KPI calculation logic. Validation and sign-off of Dupixent PSS A&R mappings by Sanofi stakeholders prior to design initiation., Requirements-to-data mapping document (Dupixent, Other TAs and Adhoc use cases) Signed-off mapping documentation Row 3: Design, Work with Sanofi Architects/ICI team on designing requirements to Data Assets mapping Finalization and preparation of DQ checks Preparation of High-level and low-level design for data objects including Data Models Source to Target Mapping Metadata tagging, Data Assets Archetypes (Requirements to new Data Assets mapping)

PSS AR KPI Enablement through ICI Snowfl.heading: Key activities and deliverables:
PSS AR KPI Enablement through ICI Snowfl.text: Columns: Key Activities, Details, Expected Outcomes/Deliverables Technical Design Document (Logical, Physical Data Model and STTM document) Minimum Metadata requirements and metadata template Finalized DQ checklist Row 4: Build, Code as per low level design and execute unit testing Metadata addition Addition of DQ checks End to end automation and orchestration configurations, Data Pipelines and code repositories Unit Test documents E2E Code review with Sanofi Row 5: SIT, Creation of Test Strategy, Test scripts Preparation / Execution of test cases considering positive and negative data scenarios End to end testing of all modules Document SIT results, defects, and co-ordinate with development team for defect resolution. Orchestration testing with dry run of end-to-end automated jobs within the application, Test Strategy Test Case scripts Test execution report (E2E) Row 6: UAT, UAT environment setup and data loads Prepare UAT Test Cases/Scripts. Collaborate with Sanofi BOS users and support them with executing & performing UAT validations. Defect Management – Defect Triaging and resolution Confirm both technical and user readiness for go-live. Define DevOps Governance and Operating Model, UAT Test Plan document UAT case scripts preparation UAT test execution report and result Paybook for Operational process and DevOps governance Row 7: Hypercare and Downstream Retrofitment, Perform hyper-care support activities, including monitor processes, provide support if needed, bug resolution and triage. Creation of required cutover and knowledge transfer documentation and KT workshops Provide documentation, oversight, SOPs, and advisory support for 6 weeks to enable downstream Power BI dashboard retrofitment by Sanofi PSS A&R HUB teams, Hypercare support and handover artifacts (SOP documents) Operations, enhancement, and KPI governance documentation. Detailed Activities, Deliverables and Ownerships.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.heading: INDIVIDUAL PROJECT AGREEMENT (IPA)
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.text: INDIVIDUAL PROJECT AGREEMENT (IPA) under. Master Services Agreement (“MSA”) Dated February 22, 2021. Customer: Effective Date of IPA: 07th Oct 2024. Expiration Date of IPA: 7th Feb 2025. Title of Project: Project Diamond –OneCI Data Quality enhancements & Medical integration. Customer Project Contact: Laurie Musolino. Vendor Project Contact: Gagan Bhatara: The following terms and conditions apply solely to work performed under this IPA and do not apply to any other IPA. The IPA is governed by the terms of the MSA dated February 22, 2021 between and ZS Associates, Inc. (“MSA”). To the extent there is any conflict between the terms and conditions of this IPA and the MSA, the MSA controls. All capitalized terms in this IPA will have the same defined meanings as set forth in the MSA. Exit Clause: With respect to the Services outlined in this SOW only, Sanofi may terminate this SOW, in whole or in part, for convenience with immediate effect. In the event this SOW is terminated for any reason, ZS shall cease performance of the Services on the date of such termination, and Sanofi shall be responsible only for payment of fees for Services performed by ZS up to termination.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.heading: In Scope
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.text: In Scope: The scope of this project is to enhance the existing OneCI setup with the following activities in-scope:

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.chunk_id: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country Medical & OneCI DQ_09-OCT-2024.docx::chunk_0005
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.file_name: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country Medical & OneCI DQ_09-OCT-2024.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country Medical & OneCI DQ_09-OCT-2024.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.source_path: C:\Users\HP\vs_studio_projects\pharma_copilot\data\sample_docs\ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country Medical & OneCI DQ_09-OCT-2024.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.heading: In Scope
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.text: Columns: Category, Activities Row 1: Medical, Conduct requirements gathering sessions(1-2) with LUCI and OneCI Team Design 2 new medical views, along with adding their source views to the OneCRM Foundation Data Mart Update the current IICS mappings across 3 layers - staging, DWH and Data Mart for OneCRM Create URS/FRS and STTM, Data models document to incorporate OneCI above country medical changes. Updates in the existing approved URS/FRS documents for OneCRM and OneCI Co-ordination with OneCI Team, LUCI Team, One CRM Row 2: Data Quality checks, Design & build for business data quality checks, according to the requirements provided by LUCI URS/FRS updates to reflect the DQ requirements, TDD updates Creation of DQ queries for required scenarios, integration of DQ module End to end testing and sign-off. Table 1: Scope of work with known enhancement details.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.heading: Design
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.text: Design: Work with Sanofi Digital and Sanofi Architects and design end to end solution for the platform. Work with the Snowflake team in defining the data models as per Snowflake best practices.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.heading: Testing Activities
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.text: Testing Activities: ZS will work with the Sanofi Digital and OneCI/LUCI teams to design, plan and conduct testing for each release to ensure that the defined requirements are successfully achieved.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.chunk_id: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country Medical & OneCI DQ_09-OCT-2024.docx::chunk_0008
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.file_name: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country Medical & OneCI DQ_09-OCT-2024.docx

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.section_title: Below activities will be conducted as part of testing phase
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.heading: Below activities will be conducted as part of testing phase:
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.text: Below activities will be conducted as part of testing phase: System Testing will ensure end to end testing of all modules within the application and will be conducted for every module. Orchestration testing with dry run of end-to-end automated jobs within the application. Testing with interfacing upstream and downstream applications to ensure no disruption in data exchanges. Validate functional scenarios and business rules. Collaborate with Sanofi Digital / OneCI / LUCI users and help them perform UAT to confirm that the application was configured as per user requirements and that data is presented accurately.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.chunk_id: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country Medical & OneCI DQ_09-OCT-2024.docx::chunk_0009
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.file_name: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country Medical & OneCI DQ_09-OCT-2024.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country Medical & OneCI DQ_09-OCT-2024.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.source_path: C:\Users\HP\vs_studio_projects\pharma_copilot\data\sample_docs\ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country Medical & OneCI DQ_09-OCT-2024.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.heading: Out of scope
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.text: Out of scope: Any new enhancements on OneCI apart from the above mentioned will be assessed separately through a change control process. Any additional DQ checks apart from the aligned scope will be out of scope for this engagement. Integration of any new data sources for OneCI/OneCRM is considered out of scope except Medicals Insights and Medical Accounts. Any changes or testing, including impact assessment of downstream applications to be handled by the respective applications. ZS will provide necessary data and support to carry out such activities by the respective applications. Any GxP processes or documentation are considered out of scope for the SOW. Any additional data sources/applications or user managed ad-hoc processes will be assessed for impact to include the changes as part of this SOW and collaborate with Sanofi to follow change control process as necessary. OneCI security control and access management tables setup and development for Power BI access control and restriction is out of scope. Existing OneCI schema to be leveraged for the build for OneCI above country views. Development new data engineering pipelines is out of scope. Assumptions.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.chunk_id: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country Medical & OneCI DQ_09-OCT-2024.docx::chunk_0010
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.file_name: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country Medical & OneCI DQ_09-OCT-2024.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country Medical & OneCI DQ_09-OCT-2024.docx

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.heading: Onboarding & Access:
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.text: Onboarding & Access: ZS resources will be onboarded to Sanofi network prior to the initiation of the project. Any delay may impact the start of the project. ZS resources will be provided Sanofi laptop or VDI within a week of project initiation to gain access to Sanofi environment to avoid any project delays. It is assumed that Sanofi Digital will provide Dev, Test, Production environment access, for all required tool stack, to ZS within a week of start of respective phase. Any delay in providing access may impact cost and timelines. This project is an enhancement work under the project diamond umbrella any assumptions related to onboarding and access mentioned in the diamond SOW will apply here.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.chunk_id: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country Medical & OneCI DQ_09-OCT-2024.docx::chunk_0011
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.file_name: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country Medical & OneCI DQ_09-OCT-2024.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country Medical & OneCI DQ_09-OCT-2024.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.source_path: C:\Users\HP\vs_studio_projects\pharma_copilot\data\sample_docs\ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country Medical & OneCI DQ_09-OCT-2024.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.heading: Infrastructure and Vendor Responsibilities:
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.text: Infrastructure and Vendor Responsibilities: Sanofi Digital will manage legal and compliance considerations for storing data in cloud environment. Infrastructure cost related to AWS / IICS /Snowflake will be managed by Sanofi Digital / Sanofi Architecture team. Sanofi architecture team will establish guidance for standards to be followed for coding and implementation and ZS resources will follow those standards. In case of urgent enhancements / fixes, Sanofi Digital will escalate to expedite migration of code from Dev to Test to Production. Sanofi Digital / Data Management will manage data contracts with data source providers. Sanofi Digital / Architecture team will be responsible for obtaining any security and compliance approvals for migrating data / code to cloud. Snowflake team will provide advisory / technical SME support in guiding around Snowflake architecture, design, data models and integration of Snowflake with IICS. It is assumed that in case of environment slowness for any tool, Sanofi Digital will escalate with the vendor teams to upgrade the environments or fix environment issues. Any delay due to environment or product related issues may impact the cost and timelines. This project is an enhancement work under the project diamond umbrella any assumptions related to infrastructure and vendor responsibilities mentioned in the diamond SOW will apply here.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.chunk_id: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country Medical & OneCI DQ_09-OCT-2024.docx::chunk_0012
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.file_name: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country Medical & OneCI DQ_09-OCT-2024.docx

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.heading: Project Delivery
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.text: Project Delivery: Any impact to the timeline due to any upstream or downstream applications/teams will be communicated to the product owners and if it impacts the project plan/delivery that will be treated as a change request outside this SOW and will go through the change management process. Sanofi Digital / Data Management will provide sign-offs on the requirements, design and other testing deliverables in a timely fashion; delays in obtaining sign-offs will directly affect the delivery timelines and cost. Existing AMS operations teams for DSS and SGDW will be available during Project Diamond implementation to clarify any questions on the processes or data in existing platforms. ZS will collaborate with Sanofi to document the process during requirements phase. All upstream and downstream applications are assumed to conduct their testing within the published timeline and any delay may impact the cost and overall timeline of the project. All downstream changes, including connectivity to Snowflake to be managed by respective system owners and/or vendors who support the operations of the downstream applications. Tech stack assumed is based on decision by Sanofi architects and as described in scope section. Any changes to the tech stack may impact the cost and timelines. ZS will be responsible for ensuring copies of all final deliverables and work products are transferred and stored on the Sanofi-designated internal shared folder unless other direction has been provided by Sanofi.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.heading: Project Delivery
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.text: Project Delivery: Any event that occurs during the project, which will require alteration of the project plan or induces a risk to quality and schedule of the project, will be deemed as a change. Activities that need design, code or testing changes that are not included as part of current scope, will be considered as enhancements. If the requested changes/enhancements are beyond the original scope of the project, and/or exceed the overall timeline of the project, a separate change request will be raised with additional cost to extend the project scope/timeline. Sanofi Project Diamond Team with guidance from digital data architecture team/tech team who will be responsible for setup / migration of environments from Dev to Test to Production. ZS will coordinate with Sanofi digital data team on scheduling or performing the deployment activities per Sanofi guidance or formal processes. This project is an enhancement work under the project diamond umbrella any assumptions related to delivery, technology stack, architecture, infrastructure/platform mentioned in the diamond SOW will apply here. Deliverables.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.text: Roles & Responsibilities: ZS understands that the success of this project is driven by right mix of people with technology and domain expertise. Following is a description of high-level roles and responsibilities of key ZS and Sanofi Resources:

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.heading: Roles & Responsibilities
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.text:
Columns: Role & Team, Responsibilities
Row 1: Onshore ZS Team, Onshore ZS Team
Row 2: Senior Business Analyst/Integration Lead, Has good domain knowledge around Sanofi data and business applications.
Engage and communicate with Sanofi stakeholders as required during product requirements definition, design, and development.
Coordination with onshore team on data findings and data analysis results
Row 3: Offshore ZS Team, Offshore ZS Team
Row 4: Technical Team Lead, Responsible for day-to-day management of project
Responsible to maintaining timelines and quality of all deliverables.
Provide oversight of technical solution design and implementation
Ensure adherence to Sanofi program management framework and standards in delivery.
Row 5: Sr. Developer, Review requirements and design and raise clarifying questions before starting build.
Review design and develop POCs along with the design team.
Review and contribute to the design with the overall solution in mind.
Lead and perform code reviews, with the understanding of overall solution and client coding standards.
Lead with the Design/build activities.
Manage day to day build activities and coordinate with offshore delivery lead to ensure progress is being made as per plan.
Row 6: Test Analyst, Prepare test cases based on scenarios defined
For an issues / concerns around design, requirement and scenarios – raise the same in defect tracker and discuss the same on daily calls 
Create test status report tool and share daily status report using the tool
During UAT phase, retest UAT defects in SIT before they are delivered to UAT for retest

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.heading: Following are some key risks and their mitigation approach
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.text: Following are some key risks and their mitigation approach: Columns: Area, Risks, Mitigation Approach Row 1: Requirements, Managing unknown requirements / processes, ZS will closely coordinate with OneCI team to help in proactively pre-empting any issues related to missed requirements and any unknown requirements uncovered after requirements phase and that impacts project timelines will be assessed through Change Control Row 2: Change Requests, Changes to scope or delivery schedule, delay project delivery and increase project management costs., Assign a single point of contact from Sanofi Digital as the product owner who is accountable for overall project plan and liaisons with other Sanofi functions. Weekly status updates meetings with the core team to highlight potential issues early to minimize impact on timeline. Periodic Steering Committee meetings to highlight / escalate issues. Any changes to scope will be assessed through Change Control Row 3: Delivery, Unavailability of downstream teams for testing, Both ZS and downstream teams to get aligned on the timeline and agree on the complete set of integration test scenarios during requirements phase, any deviations from the agreed upon timeline to be managed through Change Control Row 4: OneCI/LUCI team’s availability, Timely onboarding of managed services partner and their availability, Sanofi Digital to plan production operations team in a time bound manner.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.heading: Project Summary
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Project Summary: The scope of Project Diamond is to transforms the on-premise data warehouses DSS (Decision Support System) and SGDW (Sanofi Genzyme Datawarehouse) and build a common Cloud based Datawarehouse platform for its Genzyme Specialty and General Medicine divisions to support U.S commercial business applications. Through this initiative, Sanofi will develop an enterprise data management platform that will improve the advanced analytics capabilities and will prove to be a competitive advantage for Sanofi over its competitors. DSS & SGDW currently run on on-prem Informatica Power Center and Oracle and have grown increasingly complex and the evolution in the business needs is nearly continuous. Building the next generation data warehouse application on the cloud creates enormous opportunity to define a new model for innovation, operation and transformation to get ahead of the pace of the business. As part of this initiative, ZS will design, build and deploy all processes, that exist in current DSS and SGDW platforms, to the new cloud-based data warehouse platform based on AWS / Informatica Cloud (IICS) / Snowflake. In order to gain quicker benefits of the new platform and mitigate program execution risk, this project approaches the onboarding of business units in in an agile manner through multiple releases and ZS will follow Sanofi’s Agile delivery methodology using POD structure and use tools like JIRA and Confluence.

section_path: Project Diamond aims at providing below benefits –
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.section_key: Project Diamond aims at providing below benefits –
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.section_title: Project Diamond aims at providing below benefits –
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.heading: Project Diamond aims at providing below benefits –
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Project Diamond aims at providing below benefits –: Scalability: Build configurable frameworks and loosely coupled modules that allow faster onboarding of new datasets and scalable design for new enhancements. The design will consider future re-usability and scalability of common components to other countries. Optimization of data process: Opportunity to improve current data process and underlying business process as identified by business stakeholders in requirements and design session aligned to the global data governance framework. Accelerated Performance: Accelerate processes within the system through point to point integration across applications and effective utilization of the cloud infrastructure to process sales data faster by 25-30% or more. Data Quality: Configurable data quality engine with business DQ checks for errors that commonly affect downstream business applications that will significantly improve quality of data across the platform and delivered to downstream business applications. Eliminate limitations due to data volume: Current legacy platform limits ability to ingest complex and large volume datasets like APLD etc. These limitations will be eliminated by moving to cloud and optimizing / redesigning current data integration processes. The overall timeline of this project is 14 months – From Nov 29,2021 to Jan 31, 2023.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.heading: In Scope
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: In Scope: The scope of this project is to build a foundational data management platform on AWS cloud and to onboard all data sources and business units that are currently supported by DSS and SGDW. Below is the high-level architecture of the future cloud platform. The tech stack for the future cloud platform to be implemented as part of Project Diamond is listed below: Cloud Provider – AWS. Datawarehouse – Snowflake. Storage – AWS S3, Snowflake. Compute / ETL – Informatica Cloud (IICS), Snowflake. Orchestration – Control M. Following are the high-level activities to migrate the current on-prem DSS and SGDW to AWS / IICS / Snowflake based platform as part of Project Diamond: Setting up Cloud Foundation layer on AWS / Informatica Cloud / Snowflake following Sanofi’s best practice guidance.

PMO support for project planning and execution. Hypercare support and transition to operations. Change Management with upstream and downstream applications. Setting up Cloud Foundation layer on AWS / Informatica cloud / Snowflake following Sanofi’s best practice guidance. Project Diamond will kick-off by setting up a cloud foundation to which all data sources / business units / processes can be onboarded. Below are the activities that will be covered as part of this phase:

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Requirements & Architecture: Work with Sanofi Digital / Data Management to define the User and Functional requirements by leveraging existing DSS and SGDW documentation. Create a detailed project plan in collaboration with Sanofi Digital / Data Management, with timelines for change management with upstream / downstream applications.
