ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.heading: Data Collection Ingestion
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Data Collection Ingestion: All Data sources are expected to provide data feeds for each release in a common SFTP layer or defined AWS S3 buckets or via Snowflake data sharing mechanism for Project Diamond consumption. For Data Initiation into the new platform, data sources that provide incremental / delta files are expected to provide one-time history of the data to be initiated / processed. In case the data provider is unable to send the full feed, assuming that Quantum has the history of all the delta files sent from the source, ZS will work with the Snowflake team to migrate Historical data from Quantum. Any data transformations required to migrate Historical data will be evaluated via the change request process. Data sources that provide full history in every feed are expected to provide full files (as in BAU process) on the common SFTP layer / AWS S3 bucket. Inbound Source Data will be archived in AWS S3 Glacier as per policies and guidance from Sanofi Digital and Architecture team.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Data Pre-Processing: ETL transformations across layers will be built using Informatica Cloud (IICS) / Snowflake on AWS, along with application of business rules as defined by requirements. Configurable Business Rule Engine would be deployed to set up processes for different products/markets with respective parameters.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.heading: Data Lake Storage
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Data Lake Storage: Intermediate and Final Processed data will be stored on Snowflake based on initial architecture reviews. Snowflake would serve as persistent data storage. In addition to Snowflake, Amazon S3 would enable historical data storage layer for access to extracts to downstream systems and access to raw and processed data for historical data cycles.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.heading: Data Quality Framework
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Data Quality Framework: Set up Data Quality management framework and modules to ensure quality control across various data processing stages for each data source. Setup File level DQ Checks, Data Standardization, Business DQ checks on Inbound and Outbound files. The DQM framework also generates error summary for each processed file. The DQ checks will be stored in the DQM configuration tables. Data Publish Layer.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.section_title: Following data publish mechanisms will be made available for downstream applications
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.heading: Following data publish mechanisms will be made available for downstream applications:
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Following data publish mechanisms will be made available for downstream applications: Data Publish to CIM in .DAT format: As done in current DSS environment, files will be published to CIM in .DAT format for downstream commercial operations consumption. Data Publish to other downstream systems: Files will be published to other DSS downstream systems (Veeva, Trace, etc.) to an S3 bucket or to a SFTP where downstream systems can access the required files. Downstream applications will be able to directly connect with Snowflake to fetch data through application level connectors. Materialized Views that currently exist in SGDW, can be accessed by downstream platforms from the Data Publish Layer in Snowflake.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Orchestration & Automation: Control M would be used for orchestrating computational workflows and data processing pipelines.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.heading: Data Catalog Support
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Data Catalog Support: Work with Sanofi Data management team to create initial business metadata content for DSS/SGDW, including glossaries in the template provided by Informatica Data Catalog team and work with Data Catalog team to upload the metadata/glossaries into Informatica Data Catalog. Onboard current DSS / SGDW processes for Immunology, Oncology, Genmed, MS, RD, RBD to Cloud. Following is the tentative data onboarding plan, which will be reviewed with Sanofi and revised if needed during the requirements phase. The first release will onboard MS / RD / RBD business units to the Cloud based platform. This will also include migration of all SGDW commercial processes (de-identified patient) and reporting structures to Project Diamond. Dupixent being highest business priority, Immunology will be the second BU to be onboarded to Project Diamond, followed by Oncology. Genmed will be the final business unit to be onboarded to Cloud. All processes that exist for Genmed brands in DSS will be transformed into new Cloud based platform. Below processes, that currently exist in DSS, will be setup as part of onboarding of all business units: Processing of inbound interface data will be done via a combination of dimensions, fact data and data transformation. The dimensions and fact data listed below cover the data elements loaded into DSS/SGDW, whereas Processes/Transformations listed below are just examples of critical processes.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.heading: Data Catalog Support
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Data Catalog Support: Cleanse and stage additional data sources on the cloud that are currently loaded directly into CIM. Currently, due to limitations of legacy DSS platform, certain data sources are loaded directly into CIM. These data sources will be cleansed, standardized (joined with Customer, Product Master) and Staged on the cloud and will be available for downstream business applications. Below is the list of data sources that will be staged into Snowflake: APLD. XPD. LAAD.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: MMIT: Lifelink. DDD Monthly. Source of Business. Triggers (IQVIA, Symphony Health, ION/Intrinsiq, Komodo) Testing Activities. ZS will work with the Sanofi Digital and Data Management teams to design, plan and conduct testing for each sprint to ensure that the defined requirements are successfully achieved. Below activities will be conducted as part of testing phase.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.heading: System Testing:
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: System Testing: System Testing will ensure end to end testing of all modules within the application and will be conducted for every module. Orchestration testing with dry run of end to end automated jobs within the application.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.chunk_id: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx::chunk_0024

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.heading: Integration Testing:
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Integration Testing: Testing with interfacing upstream and downstream applications to ensure no disruption in data exchanges. Verify receipt of source extracts and upload to new platform. Validate functional scenarios and business rules.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.chunk_id: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx::chunk_0025

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.heading: Data Reconciliation Testing:
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Data Reconciliation Testing: Parallel testing with legacy DSS / SGDW platforms using same set of files to ensure acceptable match of output data from both systems. Verify Counts, Data, Data Types and Data Quality Checks.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.heading: Performance Testing:
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Performance Testing: Verify Job execution timings of processes to ensure SLAs as aligned with Sanofi. Publish report of run stats for each process.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.heading: Testing Co-ordination
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Testing Co-ordination: ZS will co-ordinate with Sanofi and Vendor teams to ensure completion of User Acceptance Testing, Testing of downstream applications and infrastructure testing.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.chunk_id: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx::chunk_0028

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.heading: User Acceptance Testing:
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: User Acceptance Testing: Collaborate with Sanofi Digital / Data Management users and help them perform UAT to confirm that the application was configured as per user requirements and that data is presented accurately. ZS will support in creation of test scenarios but the test execution will be conducted by Sanofi business users.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.chunk_id: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx::chunk_0029
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.file_name: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.heading: Downstream Applications Testing:
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Downstream Applications Testing: Co-ordinate with downstream business application users to conduct testing of their respective applications post integration with Project Diamond. The testing of downstream applications will be owned by their respective teams. ZS will co-ordinate with these downstream applications to provide necessary support to conduct their testing. Change Management. ZS will support Change Management for both upstream and downstream processes integrated with DSS / SGDW and identify and bridge any gaps for business users while undergoing the changes. Below are high level activities that will be conducted as part of change management: Create a mapping of all stakeholders impacted due to the change, along with the teams impacted. Draft and publish communications during kick-off, data reconciliation exercises and production rollout to all impacted users. Close co-ordination with the downstream applications to understand critical milestones and risks. Also, regular meetings with impacted users to communicate changes and understand / address any concerns.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.chunk_id: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx::chunk_0030
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.file_name: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_path: C:\Users\HP\vs_studio_projects\pharma_copilot\data\sample_docs\ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.section_title: Below is the major scope of the change management activities
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.heading: Below is the major scope of the change management activities
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Below is the major scope of the change management activities: Columns: S. No, User Group, Change Management Activities Row 1: 1, Downstream Commercial Operations Business Applications, Downstream commercial applications like IC, FD, FR, BI, Veeva are major consumers of data from DSS / SGDW. Below are the high-level change management activities for these applications: Data comparison and Training materials: Perform data comparison between new and legacy datasets and highlight the discrepancies, if any Prepare training materials to educate the downstream applications to start consuming data from the new cloud platform Cut-over Strategy: As downstream business applications are dependent on Frozen Quarterly process, change management and a cut-over strategy will be planned to align with the frozen timelines and to minimize the impact Communication Plan Establish the communication plan for downstream applications for awareness regarding system cut-off from legacy to new system Discuss and plan the parallel run, cutover to new system. Row 2: 2, Upstream Data Sources / Vendor Teams, Upstream sources integrate with DSS / SGDW for feeding data such as facts and dimensions. The major impact on such systems will be on the interface of integration. Interface Changes Work with the respective upstream application IT teams to coordinate the change in delivery mechanism and path Data Sources may have to feed to both legacy DSS / SGDW and Project Diamond for certain period and that will be planned and coordinated with the respective data vendor teams.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.section_title: Below is the major scope of the change management activities
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.heading: Below is the major scope of the change management activities:
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Below is the major scope of the change management activities: Hypercare Support. ZS will work with Sanofi’s managed services partner and handover the DSS / SGDW Cloud application through knowledge transition and handover activities. Hyper care support will be provided after each Release of the application along with 2 months of Hypercare support post the go-live of final Release.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.chunk_id: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx::chunk_0032
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.file_name: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_path: C:\Users\HP\vs_studio_projects\pharma_copilot\data\sample_docs\ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.section_title: Main activities of the hyper care support team will include
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.heading: Main activities of the hyper care support team will include:
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Main activities of the hyper care support team will include: Conduct and Transition KT workshops​ with Sanofi business teams and managed services partner. Heightened monitoring of the production application and relevant processes. Assisting to streamline support processes and relevant documents to hand over the production operations. Assist the managed services partner in resolving inquiries from end users on the application functionality, interface and navigations. Monitor, measure and ensure, if needed work with the managed services partner to fix any defects. Due to the phased cut-over strategy, the managed services support team for Project Diamond has to be onboarded from the start of Hypercare for first business unit (Immunology) transitioned to Project Diamond. PMO Support. ZS PMO will work in tandem with Sanofi Project/Testing Coordinator to collaborate with various Sanofi core team/downstream/Infrastructure teams and provide status updates to Sanofi core team. Below are the activities that will be covered throughout the duration of the project: Collaborate with Sanofi stakeholders to create and maintain the project plan for Project Diamond. Communicate up-to-date status of the application build to relevant IT and business stakeholders. Communicate all changes applied to the build project, any deviation to build and testing timelines. Co-ordination with all teams in all phases of the project. Create and track risks and their mitigation plan. Track changes and deviations from requirements as Change Control requests and communicate to all teams.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.chunk_id: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx::chunk_0033
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.file_name: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_path: C:\Users\HP\vs_studio_projects\pharma_copilot\data\sample_docs\ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.heading: Out of scope
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Out of scope: Infrastructure maintenance including disaster recovery/backup, security maintenance including SSO and administration will be managed by Sanofi Digital / GIS and is considered out of scope for ZS. Sanofi Project Diamond Team with guidance from digital data architecture team/tech team who will be responsible for setup / migration of environments from Dev to Test to Production. ZS will coordinate with Sanofi digital data team on scheduling or performing the deployment activities per Sanofi guidance or formal processes. As part of data catalog support, ZS will create and share initial data catalog metadata/glossaries. Setup, development and deployment of Informatica Data Catalog is out of scope and to be managed by Sanofi Data Catalog team. Ongoing updates to the metadata/glossaries will be managed by US Data Management team. With the new cloud platform, all upstream data sources will land their data into a Sanofi provisioned SFTP site or AWS S3 bucket, if direct connectivity is not available. If direct connectivity is feasible, it will be used to establish ingestion. The direct connectivity includes Snowflake Data Sharing mechanisms or accessing data from AWS S3. Building a new API setup with any application/vendor is considered out of scope for the SOW. Any new Sanofi initiatives that start after Project Diamond kicks off and impacts the Project Diamond components or timeline will be assessed separately through a change control. Any changes or testing, including impact assessment of downstream applications to be handled by the respective applications. ZS will provide necessary data and support to carry out such activities by the respective applications. Current DSS / SGDW platforms are Non-GxP, hence, any GxP processes or documentation are considered out of scope for the SOW.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.chunk_id: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx::chunk_0034
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.file_name: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_path: C:\Users\HP\vs_studio_projects\pharma_copilot\data\sample_docs\ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.heading: Out of scope
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Out of scope: Current DSS / SGDW platforms are Non-GxP, hence, any GxP processes or documentation are considered out of scope for the SOW. Section: 1.1.2 and 1.1.3 of this SOW provides the complete list of data sources and upstream/downstream applications of existing DSS / SGDW. Any additional data sources/applications or user managed ad-hoc processes will be assessed for impact to include the changes as part of this SOW and collaborate with Sanofi to follow change control process as necessary. Any processes that currently run within interfacing applications like RCM, CIM, Integration Hub, Nucleus, Veeva etc. and any changes to these except identified in section 1.1.3 are out of scope for the SOW. Decommissioning of existing DSS / SGDW platforms / servers is to be managed by Sanofi GIS and considered out of scope of the SOW. Change management due to changes introduced by other initiatives such as Product MDM are out of scope of the SOW. SGDW PDB to iCare transition is a separate initiative and any processes or patient-level data that exist in SGDW PDB are considered out of scope for the SOW. SGDW processes that are unused, decommissioned or planned to be decommissioned such as – Biologics, TM1, ENDO, Trinity/LH, all history tables (ending with HIST, HST), ENDO Reporting Structures – are considered out of scope. The scope includes development using the tech stack listed in section 1.1. Any Custom web interface setup for Data Quality Framework, Business rule engine, Data ingestion etc. is considered out of scope. Assumptions.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.heading: Onboarding & Access:
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Onboarding & Access: ZS resources will be onboarded to Sanofi network prior to the initiation of the project. Any delay may impact the start of the project. ZS resources will be provided Sanofi laptop or VDI within a week of project initiation to gain access to Sanofi environment to avoid any project delays. It is assumed that Sanofi Digital will provide Dev, Test, Production environment access, for all required tool stack, to ZS within a week of start of respective phase. Any delay in providing access may impact cost and timelines. ZS will be provided full access to data in existing DSS / SGDW / CIM platforms within a week of project Initiation for data profiling / comparison activities.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.chunk_id: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx::chunk_0036
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.file_name: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.heading: Infrastructure and Vendor Responsibilities:
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Infrastructure and Vendor Responsibilities: Sanofi Digital will manage legal and compliance considerations for storing data in cloud environment. Infrastructure cost related to AWS / IICS /Snowflake will be managed by Sanofi Digital / Sanofi Architecture team. Sanofi architecture team will establish guidance for standards to be followed for coding and implementation and ZS resources will follow those standards. In case of urgent enhancements / fixes, Sanofi Digital will escalate to expedite migration of code from Dev to Test to Production. Sanofi Digital / Data Management will manage data contracts with data source providers. Sanofi Digital / Architecture team will be responsible for obtaining any security and compliance approvals for migrating data / code to cloud. Snowflake team will provide advisory / technical SME support in guiding around Snowflake architecture, design, data models and integration of Snowflake with IICS. ZS will co-ordinate with Snowflake Migration Experts for historical data migration who will provide best practices and suitable options to migrate historical data from Oracle to Snowflake. It is assumed that in case of environment slowness for any tool, Sanofi Digital will escalate with the vendor teams to upgrade the environments or fix environment issues. Any delay due to environment or product related issues may impact the cost and timelines.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.chunk_id: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx::chunk_0037
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.file_name: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_path: C:\Users\HP\vs_studio_projects\pharma_copilot\data\sample_docs\ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.heading: Project Delivery
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Project Delivery: Sanofi Digital / Data Management will provide sign-offs on the requirements, design and other testing deliverables in a timely fashion; delays in obtaining sign-offs will directly affect the delivery timelines and cost. Existing AMS operations teams for DSS and SGDW will be available during Project Diamond implementation to clarify any questions on the processes or data in existing platforms. It is assumed that current DSS / SGDW business rules and data layouts are readily available to facilitate an efficient requirement gathering phase. For processes where there is no documentation available, ZS will collaborate with Sanofi to document the process during requirements phase. Currently, all inbound data sources into DSS and outbound data sources to platforms like Veeva, RCM are sent via Integration Hub. As Integration Hub is not part of the architecture for Project Diamond, it is assumed that all exchanges that were done via Integration Hub will be done via SFTP or AWS S3. Any data transformations currently done in Integration Hub prior to sending data to DSS are assumed to be managed by the new replacement platform for Integration Hub. If any of these assumptions change, the impact will be analyzed first and corresponding changes will be managed via change management process. Project Diamond will plan to integrate with the new US Commercial Product MDM. For timely integration, the overall timelines of new US Product MDM should sync up with Project Diamond releases and should be confirmed during Project Diamond Requirements/Design Phase. It is assumed that data models / layouts and interfacing mechanism for new US Product MDM into Project Diamond will be provided during Project Diamond Requirements/Design phase. Any challenges with the alignment of new US Product MDM timelines with Project Diamond timelines will impact the overall Project Diamond schedule and will be assessed via change control process.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.chunk_id: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx::chunk_0038
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.file_name: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.heading: Project Delivery
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Project Delivery: In case of delay with the new US Product MDM or the timelines do not sync up with Project Diamond, Project Diamond will interface with existing DSS based Product master. A decision of the same will be collectively made between Sanofi Digital and ZS and no later than the Design phase. In this case, Project Diamond will be designed to be scalable and flexible to simplify integration with new US Product MDM at a later stage which will be done through change control. The scope of the SOW includes migration of DSS and SGDW to Cloud. All applicable modules such as Business Rules Engine (BRE), Data Quality Manager (DQM) and Orchestration, will be set-up common for both DSS and SGDW. In case of establishing Snowflake to Snowflake data exchange between IQVIA and Project Diamond, it is assumed that IQVIA will apply all applicable existing rules/transformations/data filters prior to publishing the data to Sanofi. Project Management, Requirements gathering and Change Management for both DSS & SGDW to be aligned to achieve cost and resource synergies. Data exchanges between Project Diamond and the new iCare platform (replacement of SGDW PDB) will follow the standard Project Diamond data publish mechanism. All upstream and downstream applications are assumed to conduct their testing within the published timeline and any delay may impact the cost and overall timeline of the project. All downstream changes, including connectivity to Snowflake to be managed by respective system owners and/or vendors who support the operations of the downstream applications.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.chunk_id: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx::chunk_0039
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.file_name: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.heading: Project Delivery
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Project Delivery: All downstream changes, including connectivity to Snowflake to be managed by respective system owners and/or vendors who support the operations of the downstream applications. In case of any enhancements done to DSS / SGDW post initiation of Project Diamond, Sanofi Digital will track and manage them. ZS will collaborate with Sanofi to determine if a change request is required to reapply these changes in the cloud environment. Any minor enhancements such as bug fixes, minor critical business requirements (less than 80 hours), up to a reasonable limit and without extending the project timeline, will be covered as part of the project. Any major changes such as onboarding a new data source or addition of a new BU etc. will go through change control process. Tech stack assumed is based on decision by Sanofi architects and as described in scope section. Any changes to the tech stack may impact the cost and timelines. ZS will be responsible for ensuring copies of all final deliverables and work products are transferred and stored on the Sanofi-designated internal shared folder, unless other direction has been provided by Sanofi. Any event that occurs during the project, which will require alteration of the project plan or induces a risk to quality and schedule of the project, will be deemed as a change. Activities that need design, code or testing changes that are not included as part of current scope, will be considered as enhancements. If the requested changes/enhancements are beyond the original scope of the project, and/or exceed the overall timeline of the project, a separate change request will be raised with additional cost to extend the project scope/timeline.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.heading: Transition / Cut-over to new platform:
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Transition / Cut-over to new platform: Until the full cutover is complete, inbound data source providers will Publish the files at a common location that can be utilized by both DSS and the New Cloud Platform for parallel processing. To ensure minimal impact to downstream business applications, timelines for go-live of Sprints / Releases may have to be adjusted based on Quarterly Frozen Cycle deliveries. This will be planned as part of the requirements and planning phase. To ensure minimal impact to downstream business applications, transition of all downstream applications to be planned as part of requirements & planning phase to ensure that they are ready for cut-over to new platform according to project releases or at the final cut-over of Project Diamond. Deliverables.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.heading: Timelines
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Timelines: In order to speed up the delivery of work products, to cause minimal interruption to downstream applications and to achieve a flexible delivery model, this project follows agile approach for implementation. The requirements will be identified and defined during project initiation as part of the Foundation phase and post the Foundation phase, each release will be implemented through multiple sprints. As indicated in the timeline diagram below, the foundational cloud platform is built first, then the data sources for respective business units are onboarded through multiple releases. A tentative priority order of onboarding BUs is mentioned below, which will be reviewed with Sanofi and revised if needed within the overall timeline of the project. Foundation: This phase includes collecting overall requirements, defining the architecture and setting up the cloud foundation in AWS / Snowflake / Informatica Cloud based on best practice guidance from Sanofi. Release 1: This phase includes onboarding of MS/ RD/ RBD processes that exist in DSS and SGDW and building reporting structures that currently exist in SGDW. Release 2: This phase includes onboarding of Immunology and Oncology processes. Release 3: This phase includes onboarding of Genmed processes. Through above releases all processes that currently exist in DSS / SGDW for above Business Units will be transformed and migrated to the new cloud-based platform. The following table shows implementation timelines. The overall timeline is– From Nov 29,2021 to Jan 31, 2023. As indicated, each release will be implemented through multiple sprints. The sprint structure will be defined in collaboration with Sanofi during the requirements phase. The TA deployment schedule may change as the project team will define the deployment list based on priority, readiness and resource availability in requirements phase, without changing the overall timelines.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.heading: Roles & Responsibilities
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Roles & Responsibilities: ZS understands that the success of this project is driven by right mix of people with technology and domain expertise. ZS is committed to deploy a technical team well-versed in the technologies such as AWS, Snowflake and Informatica to ensure success for Project Diamond. If at any time, the services provided by a ZS resource is not in accordance with Sanofi’s expectations, ZS will have maximum of 2 weeks to correct the matter. In the even it is not resolved, Sanofi has the right to request a replacement and ZS will take immediate action. ZS will notify Sanofi of any lead time required to replace the resource for full transparency. To ensure that Project Diamond design and architecture are being deployed as per best practices, ZS will engage with its AWS, Snowflake and Informatica partners as necessary and at various points so that Sanofi can get maximum benefits out of the technical tools. Following is a description of high-level roles and responsibilities of key ZS and Sanofi Resources:

manage product backlog and track progress Row 7: Offshore ZS Team, Offshore ZS Team Row 8: Offshore Sanofi Delivery Lead, Accountable for the delivery of the all the Sanofi projects from India Ensures that all the Sanofi workstreams are well connected and share learnings Row 9: Technical Team Lead, Responsible for day-to-day management of project Responsible to maintaining timelines and quality of all deliverables Provide oversight of technical solution design and implementation Ensure adherence to Sanofi program management framework and standards in delivery Row 10: Sr. Developer, Review requirements and design and raise clarifying questions before starting build.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.section_title: Following are some key risks and their mitigation approach
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.heading: Following are some key risks and their mitigation approach
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text: Following are some key risks and their mitigation approach: Columns: Area, Risks, Mitigation Approach Row 1: Requirements, Managing unknown requirements / processes, ZS will closely coordinate with Sanofi Data Management team to help in proactively pre-empting any issues related to missed requirements and any unknown requirements uncovered after requirements phase and that impacts project timelines will be assessed through Change Control Row 2: Onboarding, Delay in Onboarding of ZS resources to Sanofi network and Project Diamond tool stack, Sanofi Digital to help initiate onboarding of ZS resources prior to initiation of the project to avoid any delays to Project Timelines Row 3: Change Requests, Changes to scope or delivery schedule, delay project delivery and increase project management costs, Assign a single point of contact from Sanofi Digital as the product owner who is accountable for overall project plan and liaisons with other Sanofi functions Weekly status updates meetings with the core team to highlight potential issues early to minimize impact on timeline. Periodic Steering Committee meetings to highlight / escalate issues Any changes to scope will be assessed through Change Control Row 4: Infrastructure, Unavailability of Sanofi GIS for code migration across environments or resolving infrastructure issues, Sanofi Digital /GIS will identify appropriate backup resources to resolve infrastructure issues as well as expedite code/data migration requests Row 5: Delivery, Unavailability of downstream teams for testing, Both ZS and downstream teams to get aligned on the timeline and agree on the complete set of integration test scenarios during requirements phase, any deviations from the agreed upon timeline to be managed through Change Control Row 6: Transition, Timely onboarding of managed services partner and their availability, Sanofi Digital to plan production operations team in a time bound manner and onboard them prior to the first release of BU (Immunology) into Project Diamond.

Columns: Term, Description Row 1: API, Application Program Interface Row 2: AWS, Amazon Web services Row 3: BAU, Business as usual Row 4: BIA, Business Intelligence & Analytics; refers to DW&BI applications in Sanofi ecosystem Row 5: BRE, Business Rule Engine Row 6: BU, Business Unit Row 7: CARS, Contract Administration and Reporting System Row 8: CIM, Customer Information Management; refers to the system with SAS datasets that provide analytics to downstream commercial operation applications Row 9: DDD, Drug Distribution Data. Sales data of direct and indirect sales at the zip code level (retail) or at the outlet level (non-retail/hospital) every month. Row 10: DQF, Data Quality Framework Row 11: DSS, Decision Support System. The module of Quantum that deals with sales and marketing information and the analysis of that information to assist in creating forecasts. It also stages the raw files from the various source systems. Row 20: Liquid Hub, Specialty Pharmacy Data Aggregator and Source Provider Row 21: MS, Multiple Sclerosis Row 22: ONC, Oncology Row 23: PDB, Patient Database Row 24: PMO, Project Management Office Row 25: RBD, Rare blood disorder Row 26: RCM, Regional Customer Master Row 27: RD, Rare disease Row 28: S3, Simple storage services.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.heading: INDIVIDUAL PROJECT AGREEMENT (IPA)
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.text: INDIVIDUAL PROJECT AGREEMENT (IPA) under. Master Services Agreement (“MSA”) Dated February 22, 2021. Customer: Effective Date of IPA: 22nd July 2024. Expiration Date of IPA: 27th September 2024. Title of Project: Project Diamond – OneCI enhancements. Customer Project Contact: Laurie Musolino. Vendor Project Contact: Gagan Bhatara: The following terms and conditions apply solely to work performed under this IPA and do not apply to any other IPA. The IPA is governed by the terms of the MSA dated February 22, 2021 between and ZS Associates, Inc. (“MSA”). To the extent there is any conflict between the terms and conditions of this IPA and the MSA, the MSA controls. All capitalized terms in this IPA will have the same defined meanings as set forth in the MSA. Exit Clause: With respect to the Services outlined in this SOW only, Sanofi may terminate this SOW, in whole or in part, for convenience with immediate effect. In the event this SOW is terminated for any reason, ZS shall cease performance of the Services on the date of such termination, and Sanofi shall be responsible only for payment of fees for Services performed by ZS up to termination.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.chunk_id: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhancements.docx::chunk_0002

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhancements.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.heading: Project Summary
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.text: Project Summary: As part of the ongoing project diamond releases planned for this year, there is a need from the OneCI team to update the existing OneCI Global views to include the new and missing fields and update business rules as per the documentation shared by OneCI/LUCI Team. Additionally, there is an assumed total capacity of 90 hours: on top of the scoped list of enhancements mentioned in Table -1 under the scope section. Any additional enhancements on top of the scope will be added to the backlog, prioritized (with the product owners) and implemented within the allocated 90 hours capacity (to be planned during of sprint-3 that is inclusive of development, testing, UAT and deployment activities over a period of 3 weeks). The type of enhancements assumed in sprint-3 are minor attribute-level or business logic updates, no new data ingestion or major architectural changes are assumed to be in scope. The overall timeline of this scope of work is 10 weeks – From 22nd July 2024 through 27th September 2024.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.chunk_id: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhancements.docx::chunk_0003
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.file_name: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhancements.docx

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.heading: In Scope
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.text: In Scope: The scope of this project is to enhance the existing OneCI setup with the following activities in-scope:

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.chunk_id: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhancements.docx::chunk_0004
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.file_name: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhancements.docx

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.heading: In Scope
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.text:
Columns: Enhancement Subject Area, Enhancement description, Sprint
Row 1: Territory, Get territory hierarchy from Valign instead of CRM, 2
Row 2: Event, Updates in the format attribute, 1
Row 3: Interactions, Implementation of submitted interactions logic, 1
Row 4: Interactions, Integration of employee information in RTE data, 1
Row 5: Field Days, Remapping of Time of Territory values based on US CRM, 1
Row 6: Field Days, Correcting calendar and calling days logic, 1
Row 7: OCCP, Updates in the is_occp flag based on values flowing in US CRM, 1
Row 8: , Employee, same employee for all lines, 1
Row 9: Coaching, Coaching report data filtering on Survey Type to avoid duplicates, 2
Row 10: RTE, Updates in the first/last emails logics, 1
Row 11: Product, Updates in logic for disease code, 1
Row 12: CLM, Changes in CLM Interactions logics, 1
Row 13: New attributes addition, Integration of 10 new fields based on new business requirements, 2
Row 14: New attributes addition, Incorporation of 53 new timestamp attributes across 26 views, 2

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.heading: Design
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.text: Design: Work with Sanofi Digital and Sanofi Architects and design end to end solution for the platform. Work with the Snowflake team in defining the data models as per Snowflake best practices.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.chunk_id: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhancements.docx::chunk_0007
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.file_name: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhancements.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhancements.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.heading: Testing Activities
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.text: Testing Activities: ZS will work with the Sanofi Digital and OneCI/LUCI teams to design, plan and conduct testing for each sprint to ensure that the defined requirements are successfully achieved.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.chunk_id: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhancements.docx::chunk_0008
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.file_name: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhancements.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhancements.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.section_title: Below activities will be conducted as part of testing phase
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.heading: Below activities will be conducted as part of testing phase:
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.text: Below activities will be conducted as part of testing phase: System Testing will ensure end to end testing of all modules within the application and will be conducted for every module. Orchestration testing with dry run of end-to-end automated jobs within the application. Testing with interfacing upstream and downstream applications to ensure no disruption in data exchanges. Validate functional scenarios and business rules. Collaborate with Sanofi Digital / OneCI / LUCI users and help them perform UAT to confirm that the application was configured as per user requirements and that data is presented accurately.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.chunk_id: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhancements.docx::chunk_0009
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.file_name: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhancements.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhancements.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.heading: Out of scope
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.text: Out of scope: Any new enhancements on OneCI apart from the above mentioned will be assessed separately through a change control process. DQ checks development hasn't been scoped in this timeline as we still need a thorough discussion on what DQ checks need to be implemented across different layers. Integration of any new data sources for OneCI/OneCRM is considered out of scope. Any changes or testing, including impact assessment of downstream applications to be handled by the respective applications. ZS will provide necessary data and support to carry out such activities by the respective applications. Any GxP processes or documentation are considered out of scope for the SOW. Any additional data sources/applications or user managed ad-hoc processes will be assessed for impact to include the changes as part of this SOW and collaborate with Sanofi to follow change control process as necessary. OneCI security control and access management tables setup and development for Power BI access control and restriction is out of scope. Assumptions.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhancements.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.heading: Onboarding & Access:
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.text: Onboarding & Access: ZS resources will be onboarded to Sanofi network prior to the initiation of the project. Any delay may impact the start of the project. ZS resources will be provided Sanofi laptop or VDI within a week of project initiation to gain access to Sanofi environment to avoid any project delays. It is assumed that Sanofi Digital will provide Dev, Test, Production environment access, for all required tool stack, to ZS within a week of start of respective phase. Any delay in providing access may impact cost and timelines. This project is an enhancement work under the project diamond umbrella any assumptions related to onboarding and access mentioned in the diamond SOW will apply here.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.chunk_id: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhancements.docx::chunk_0011
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.file_name: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhancements.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhancements.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.heading: Infrastructure and Vendor Responsibilities:
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.text: Infrastructure and Vendor Responsibilities: Sanofi Digital will manage legal and compliance considerations for storing data in cloud environment. Infrastructure cost related to AWS / IICS /Snowflake will be managed by Sanofi Digital / Sanofi Architecture team. Sanofi architecture team will establish guidance for standards to be followed for coding and implementation and ZS resources will follow those standards. In case of urgent enhancements / fixes, Sanofi Digital will escalate to expedite migration of code from Dev to Test to Production. Sanofi Digital / Data Management will manage data contracts with data source providers. Sanofi Digital / Architecture team will be responsible for obtaining any security and compliance approvals for migrating data / code to cloud. Snowflake team will provide advisory / technical SME support in guiding around Snowflake architecture, design, data models and integration of Snowflake with IICS. It is assumed that in case of environment slowness for any tool, Sanofi Digital will escalate with the vendor teams to upgrade the environments or fix environment issues. Any delay due to environment or product related issues may impact the cost and timelines. This project is an enhancement work under the project diamond umbrella any assumptions related to infrastructure and vendor responsibilities mentioned in the diamond SOW will apply here.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.chunk_id: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhancements.docx::chunk_0012
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.file_name: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhancements.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhancements.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.heading: Project Delivery
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.text: Project Delivery: Sprint – 3 assumed 90 hours of additional enhancements across 3 weeks, on top of the list of enhancements mentioned under the scope section. Any additional enhancements on top of the scope will be added to the backlog, prioritized (with the product owners) and implemented within the allocated 90 hours capacity, if there are enhancements that exceed the 90 hour capacity and have been prioritized those will need to be treated as a change order. The type of enhancements assumed in sprint-3 are minor attribute-level or business logic updates, no new data ingestion or major architectural changes are assumed to be in scope. Any impact to the timeline due to any upstream or downstream applications/teams will be communicated to the product owners and if it impacts the project plan/delivery that will be treated as a change request outside this SOW and will go through the change management process. Sanofi Digital / Data Management will provide sign-offs on the requirements, design and other testing deliverables in a timely fashion; delays in obtaining sign-offs will directly affect the delivery timelines and cost. Existing AMS operations teams for DSS and SGDW will be available during Project Diamond implementation to clarify any questions on the processes or data in existing platforms. ZS will collaborate with Sanofi to document the process during requirements phase. All upstream and downstream applications are assumed to conduct their testing within the published timeline and any delay may impact the cost and overall timeline of the project. All downstream changes, including connectivity to Snowflake to be managed by respective system owners and/or vendors who support the operations of the downstream applications. Tech stack assumed is based on decision by Sanofi architects and as described in scope section. Any changes to the tech stack may impact the cost and timelines.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.text: Project Delivery: Tech stack assumed is based on decision by Sanofi architects and as described in scope section. Any changes to the tech stack may impact the cost and timelines. ZS will be responsible for ensuring copies of all final deliverables and work products are transferred and stored on the Sanofi-designated internal shared folder unless other direction has been provided by Sanofi. Any event that occurs during the project, which will require alteration of the project plan or induces a risk to quality and schedule of the project, will be deemed as a change. Activities that need design, code or testing changes that are not included as part of current scope, will be considered as enhancements. If the requested changes/enhancements are beyond the original scope of the project, and/or exceed the overall timeline of the project, a separate change request will be raised with additional cost to extend the project scope/timeline. Sanofi Project Diamond Team with guidance from digital data architecture team/tech team who will be responsible for setup / migration of environments from Dev to Test to Production. ZS will coordinate with Sanofi digital data team on scheduling or performing the deployment activities per Sanofi guidance or formal processes. This project is an enhancement work under the project diamond umbrella any assumptions related to delivery, technology stack, architecture, infrastructure/platform mentioned in the diamond SOW will apply here. Deliverables.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.text: Following are some key risks and their mitigation approach: Columns: Area, Risks, Mitigation Approach Row 1: Requirements, Managing unknown requirements / processes, ZS will closely coordinate with OneCI team to help in proactively pre-empting any issues related to missed requirements and any unknown requirements uncovered after requirements phase and that impacts project timelines will be assessed through Change Control Row 2: Change Requests, Changes to scope or delivery schedule, delay project delivery and increase project management costs., Assign a single point of contact from Sanofi Digital as the product owner who is accountable for overall project plan and liaisons with other Sanofi functions. Weekly status updates meetings with the core team to highlight potential issues early to minimize impact on timeline. Periodic Steering Committee meetings to highlight / escalate issues. Any changes to scope will be assessed through Change Control Row 3: Delivery, Unavailability of downstream teams for testing, Both ZS and downstream teams to get aligned on the timeline and agree on the complete set of integration test scenarios during requirements phase, any deviations from the agreed upon timeline to be managed through Change Control Row 4: OneCI/LUCI team’s availability, Timely onboarding of managed services partner and their availability, Sanofi Digital to plan production operations team in a time bound manner.

ZS SoW Dupixent PSS 2026 Q1 Enhancements.text: • Operations support is out of scope for this SOW • Creating training videos, infographic content 2.3 Assumptions • For the engagement, the ZS team will be utilizing the PV file, dispense file, PSS View file and other required datasets to develop data views and reports • The data provided by upstream will be quality checked by the upstream teams and will ensure the quality of the same • Delays arising from data availability or other dependencies may affect the timelines of individual deliverables, potentially impacting the overall project schedule and the associated professional fees. • The Sanofi PSS A&R team will be responsible for finalizing business rules • Sanofi will ensure the availability of relevant stakeholders to share business requirements, rules, and other project-related guidance. • The project timeline assumes that all dependencies and prerequisites are met as scheduled • The entire ZS team (except the UI/UX SME) will be based out of ZS India offices • India ZS team will overlap with the Sanofi stakeholders during US ET AM hours • ZS resources will be onboarded to the Sanofi network prior to the initiation of the project. Any delay may impact on the project’s start • ZS will get the required support from Sanofi to address and open queries and respond to information required by ZS teams • Any reduction in hours owing to Offshore holidays will not be carried forward to the following weeks and will account for reduction in the week of occurrence • Current scope and budget estimations include activities defined under the section 2.1.

The proposed plan is as below: • The enhancements will be bundled in 4-week releases, ensuring incremental transition to HUB • For PSS Performance Dashboard, the launch cycle will be longer (8 weeks including requirements finalization, data and dashboard build, testing, Launch and Hypercare) followed by 4-week release cycles for enhancements • The requirements for the next release cycle are to be finalized a week before the commencement of upcoming release cycle to ensure smooth releases. • Development, Testing and Hypercare phases for all dashboards will end on 1st May. Subsequent weeks will be dedicated for shadow support to HUB team for smooth transition. ZS team will operate in a limited capacity to provide shadow support 3. ZSA_Sanofi_SOW_CR.docx.heading: Change Order No. 1 to Individual Project Agreement- PO #E003720017 under Master Services Agreement
ZSA_Sanofi_SOW_CR.docx.text:
Change Order No. 1 to Individual Project Agreement- PO #E003720017 under Master Services Agreement

This Change Order No. 1 (“Change Order”) to PO #E003720017, retroactively effective as of January 31, 2023 (“Effective Date”) is by and between Sanofi-Aventis US LLC., (“Client”) and ZS Associates Inc. (“Provider”). WHEREAS, Client and Provider entered into that certain Master Services Agreement dated as of January 08, 2024, as amended to date (“Agreement”); WHEREAS, Client and Provider entered into that certain Individual Project Agreement (the “IPA”), also referred to as PO #E003720017, effective as of January 01, 2024 and expiring December 31, 2024. WHEREAS, Client and Provider now wish to amend the terms of PO #E003720017 in order to extend the Services and include additional scope.

section_path: NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the foregoing and for other good and valuable

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the foregoing and for other good and valuable

consideration, receipt of which is hereby acknowledged, the Parties hereto mutually agree to amend the IPA as follows:

ZSA_Sanofi_SOW_CR.docx.heading: Exit Clause:
ZSA_Sanofi_SOW_CR.docx.text: Exit Clause: With respect to the Services outlines in this SOW only, Sanofi may terminate this SOW, in whole or in part, for convenience to terminating immediately, providing 10 days’ notice. In the event this SOW is terminated for any reason, ZS shall cease the performance of the Services on the date of such termination, and Sanofi shall be responsible only for the payment of fees for Services performed by ZS up to the termination date.

ZSA_Sanofi_SOW_CR.docx.heading: Project Extension
ZSA_Sanofi_SOW_CR.docx.text: Project Extension: Customer Project Contact is Susan Wisnieski. Vendor Project Contact is Gagan Bhatara. The IPA, PO #E003720017 is amended to extend the contract end date. Consequently, the new end date of PO #E003720017 is December 31, 2024. Additional Services.

ZSA_Sanofi_SOW_CR.docx.heading: Scope
ZSA_Sanofi_SOW_CR.docx.text: Scope: The following scope items will be performed between January 02, 2024, to December 31, 2024: Development of US Commercial data for Tzield till Foundation with no feedback to CIM. Will closely coordinating with the ZS Dupixent Analytics team to provide guidance on standards for the Snowflake and Diamond build. This collaboration includes conducting an assessment and performing minor implementation work on the Diamond foundation side. Diamond team is responsible for offering subject matter expertise (SME) support to the ZS Analytics team for the Databricks migration. Diamond team is to provide offshore PMO support for the Dupixent release. Fitment of One Product and VDS 3.0 in GenMed release along with Change management for One Product and VDS 3.0. Diamond team responsibility to undertake One Product Foundation implementation. Retrofit of One Product & One CRM for Dupixet/Kevzara and RBD is to be carried out as part of the Oncology release.

For all other Delivery Milestones listed below beginning on or after January 1, 2024, Provider will work with Client team to execute the described scope activities and deliverables below:

ZSA_Sanofi_SOW_CR.docx.heading: Scope Activities and Deliverables
ZSA_Sanofi_SOW_CR.docx.text: Scope Activities and Deliverables: Major defects: Major defects are those which substantially prevent the deliverable from functioning or performing in accordance with the acceptance criteria set forth herein for such deliverable. Major defects materially impact Client’s ability to use the deliverable and/or cause issues with the trust and reliability of the deliverable and its output. Critical defects: Critical defects are the most serious type of defects. Critical defects render the deliverable unusable in accordance with the acceptance criteria set forth herein for such deliverable and/or could cause harm to Client. Critical defects would put Client at serious risk of liability issues, lawsuits and compliance issues. The start and end dates specified above might change as per ongoing discussions between ZS and Sanofi and they will be formalized through a change order. ZS will provide hypercare support for 2 weeks and shadow two full data refreshes as per source data frequency after the Go Live of every release or until all Critical or Major defects reported within this hypercare window have been resolved. The above schedule of the delivery milestones is for the complete duration of Change Order, which may have to be revisited based on the actual scenarios and any dependencies. In such a scenario both the parties will agree on the revised plan through change control process. In case any delay occurs due to reasons not related to ZS and impacts the deliverable milestone, the milestone payment schedule will be managed through change control process with alignment from Sanofi and ZS leadership.

ZSA_Sanofi_SOW_CR.docx.heading: Diamond Analytics Roadmap
ZSA_Sanofi_SOW_CR.docx.text: Diamond Analytics Roadmap: Out of Scope: The out-of-scope section includes everything from the original Diamond IPA in addition to the following: Coordination with IC / FD / Reporting team for data bricks migration is out of scope. VDS 2.0 not considered as part of Tzield Foundation as VDS 3.0 will be built as part of Oncology/Genmeds release. ZS PMO Support outside Dupixent release. Any new Sanofi initiatives that start after Project Diamond Analytics kicks off and impacts the Project Diamond Analytics components or foundation components timeline will be assessed separately through a change control. Any upcoming projects / changes / new source ingestion / new product launch in the CIM / Diamond environment after initiation of this project are considered out of scope and will be estimated separately. New source ingestion or product launch not in current CIM/Diamond environment are considered out of scope and will be estimated separately. Reporting Dashboards are out of scope: Development and transformation of reports that are currently based on CIM, report business logic, reporting data models, reporting dashboards or dashboard mock-ups are considered out of scope. Technical Decommissioning of existing CIM / servers is to be managed by Sanofi Digital and considered out of scope of the SOW. Change management and Integration to Diamond Analytics due to changes introduced by other initiatives such as Power BI, OneCRM, Product MDM are out of scope of the SOW. Any Custom web interface/UI setup for Data Quality Framework, Business rule engine, Data ingestion is considered out of scope. User account creation, provisioning of the technology stack provisioning, and any relevant activities for Diamond Plus - Analytics are assumed to managed by Sanofi Digital and is considered out of scope.

ZSA_Sanofi_SOW_CR.docx.heading: Diamond Analytics Roadmap
ZSA_Sanofi_SOW_CR.docx.text: Diamond Analytics Roadmap: User account creation, provisioning of the technology stack provisioning, and any relevant activities for Diamond Plus - Analytics are assumed to managed by Sanofi Digital and is considered out of scope. Assessment or evaluation of tools and products to support Diamond Plus – Analytics architecture, design, build and installation of infrastructure components is considered out of scope. Readiness and design of Diamond Plus – Analytics architecture and infrastructure will be owned and managed by Sanofi. Current CIM platform is non-GxP, hence any GxP processes or documentation are considered out of scope. Assumptions The Assumption section includes everything from the original Diamond SoW in addition to the following: Implementation / migration of Dupixent processes from Databricks to Snowflake to be done by Analytics teams. Diamond team to provide SME support during assessment phase to Analytics team on Databricks migration. Minor implementation work to be one by Diamond team in Diamond Foundation based upon data bricks assessment outcome in Dupixent release. Diamond team to review the start time for the GenMed release to avoid any heavy parallelism. Tzield Analytics processing to be done as part of GenMed release. VDS 3.0 to be implemented for Tzield foundation. The chosen IDE product (One AI or otherwise) will be responsible for best practices / providing advisory / technical support for components and architecture. Changes to Sanofi defined technology stack will be reviewed with Sanofi and ZS leadership to evaluate impact on the project. Changes to the project plan after finalization during the Planning phase will be reviewed with Sanofi and ZS leadership to evaluate impact on the project timelines, effort, and cost. ZS will provide the high-level estimate (effort, cost and time) in agreed upon number of days to Sanofi.

ZSA_Sanofi_SOW_CR.docx.heading: Diamond Analytics Roadmap
ZSA_Sanofi_SOW_CR.docx.text: Diamond Analytics Roadmap: Existing operations teams for CIM will be available during Project’s implementation to clarify any questions on the processes or data in existing platforms. It is assumed that current DSS / SGDW / CIM business rules and data layouts are readily available to facilitate an efficient requirement gathering phase. For processes where there is no documentation available, ZS will collaborate with Sanofi to document the process during requirements phase. Given that Diamond Omni Channel migration will be a parallel program, ZS Diamond team will work collaboratively with the Omni team and ensure Diamond is integrated with the new Omni Channel platform as per the finalized Sanofi architecture and strategy. Also, Diamond team will keep its project plan aligned with the Omni Channel project plan to ensure seamless integrations. ZS Diamond will support integration and testing with scope-defined dependent systems while Diamond team will be responsible for testing the Diamond side of the integration, each of those dependent system teams will be responsible for performing testing and any integration required within their system. Diamond will provide these systems with integration specifications and support E2E testing with data exchanges & corresponding validation. Also, if testing support is required beyond Diamond Analytics hyper care period, it will be evaluated / estimated separately depending on the nature of the required support. This engagement is based on a fixed effort and fixed price for the given scope and duration of the engagement. Any extension to scope or timeline beyond timeline stated in this Change Order, which is December 31, 2024, will be reviewed by Sanofi and ZS leadership to evaluate the impact and may require a change order to accommodate the additional timeline and effort. It is assumed that PMO activities are owned and managed by Sanofi Digital team.

ZSA_Sanofi_SOW_CR.docx.heading: Diamond Analytics Roadmap
ZSA_Sanofi_SOW_CR.docx.text: Diamond Analytics Roadmap: It is assumed that PMO activities are owned and managed by Sanofi Digital team. For environment performance issues of any tool, system, or product required by Diamond Plus – Analytics engagement, Sanofi Digital will escalate with the product or vendor teams to address the issue. Any delay due to environment or product related issues may impact the planned release timelines of the project. Sanofi Architecture team will establish best practices and coding standards to be followed for implementation which will be followed by ZS team. Sanofi Digital and/or Architecture team will provide signoffs on the design, development, and testing deliverables in a timely fashion; delays in obtaining signoffs may affect the delivery timelines and effort. Design and build will be limited to requirements defined during each TA release. If new requirements or business rules are defined post finalization or during/post UAT which will impact the overall project timeline and effort, ZS will complete the impact assessment within an agreed upon timeline, and present to Sanofi and ZS leadership teams for decision making. ZS will support Sanofi BOS users in creation of UAT test scenarios and fully support users in training and UAT, however UAT execution will be conducted by Sanofi BOS users. To ensure minimal impact to downstream business applications, transition of all downstream applications to be planned as part of requirements & planning phase to ensure that they are ready for cut-over to new platform according to project releases. Project team will work with Sanofi University team to upload the training material for users migrating from CIM to Diamond Plus – Analytics. Downstream changes and testing to be managed by respective system owners and/or vendors who support the operations of the downstream applications.

ZSA_Sanofi_SOW_CR.docx.heading: Diamond Analytics Roadmap
ZSA_Sanofi_SOW_CR.docx.text: Diamond Analytics Roadmap: Downstream changes and testing to be managed by respective system owners and/or vendors who support the operations of the downstream applications. ZS will work with Sanofi’s managed services partner and handover the Diamond Plus - Analytics processes through knowledge transition and handover activities. Two weeks of Hyper care support will be provided after each TA release and two full data refreshes as per source data frequency Hyper Care will only be exited if activities listed in the Hypercare Scope section for support team have been completed and there are no critical or major issues. Transition of all downstream applications will be planned and managed by individual application owners to new platform according to project releases or at the final cut-over of Project Diamond Plus - Analytics to ensure minimal impact. Current scope and budget estimations include activities defined under scope section. As part of change management ZS will support & train users to configure user managed program on the Sanofi identified low code no code tool. Post completion of the Hypercare period for a specific release, any changes or enhancements will be managed through change control process.

ZSA_Sanofi_SOW_CR.docx.text: Key Vendor Resources, Roles, and Allocation: The onshore resources will be working from US in the EST time zone and the offshore resources will be working from ZS India offices. ZS onshore and offshore resource holidays/leave policy will be in accordance with the resource location and ZS policy. 2023 holiday schedule for ZS US & India offices mentioned in Appendix A.

ZSA_Sanofi_SOW_CR.docx.heading: Budget
ZSA_Sanofi_SOW_CR.docx.text: Budget: The price and invoicing schedule for this Change Order is defined in the Scope Activities and Deliverables section. Fees to be invoiced on a Fixed Price basis, as per the above table that represents the invoice schedule that are tied to the delivery milestone. ZS will invoice Sanofi in accordance with the Invoice Dates set forth in Section 2. The total Project fees authorized under this CO shall not exceed the amount of USD $3,992,895 4,787,895 ($2,400,000 Diamond Analytics (to be invoiced) + $846,497 Remaining Diamond Phase 1 (to be invoiced) + $746,398 (invoiced for the completed Diamond Phase 1 releases) +$795,000 Diamond Analytics Replanning Estimates (to be invoiced) without the prior written consent of Sanofi and supported by a mutually executed change request. All amounts quoted in this IPA are exclusive of any taxes, expenses, etc. All amounts mentioned in this IPA are exclusive of taxes and the same shall be invoiced at actuals as and when applicable.

ZSA_Sanofi_SOW_CR.docx.heading: Service Level Agreement – Timeliness
ZSA_Sanofi_SOW_CR.docx.text: Service Level Agreement – Timeliness: The schedule variation % of time measures for this penalty shall be determined on the ratio of the difference between the actual start and end date to the difference between the planned start date and end date. The Schedule Variation % will be calculated basis of the delayed delivery milestone. ZS shall complete the milestones within a schedule variation of 100% of the estimated schedule (unless Sanofi and ZS mutually agree on any deviations from the schedule). In the event the schedule variation exceeds 100%, ZS shall be liable to pay service credit. The service credit shall apply 1% each week of the delayed delivery milestone. In no event shall the total amount of service level credits shall exceed, 12% of the delivery milestone (the “Total Service Credit Cap”). The service credit shall only be applicable if the delay in the timeline is solely due to the fault of ZS, and such credit shall be the sole and exclusive remedy to Sanofi. The applicability of service credit will be discussed regularly in the operational committee between Sanofi and ZS.

ZSA_Sanofi_SOW_CR.docx.heading: Accommodating Sanofi Internalization:
ZSA_Sanofi_SOW_CR.docx.text: Accommodating Sanofi Internalization: If during the program Sanofi internalizes resources (Data engineers, Data Analyst, etc.), the Parties will renegotiate workload, RACI & Cost impact in good faith. The change will be applied on release level and Sanofi will provide 10 business days’ notice to ZS for resource roll-off. All such changes will be documented in a separate change order. IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the Parties have executed this Change Order effective as of the Effective Date set forth above. Sanofi-Aventis US LLC ZS Associates, Inc.
